clean_body: Honor veterans with a cold beer Your gift ensures every veteran has a place at the table at American Valor | | | Peter, This weekend, nearly one hundred American veterans — from World War II to the War on Terror — are gathering in Washington, D.C. at the 28th Annual Veterans Conference. They aren’t just talking to each other. They’re sitting across the table from hundreds of cadets and midshipmen, America’s next generation of military leaders. Peter, you can make sure that table is set. It's a simple but meaningful way to honor these heroes at this year's conference. Moments like these shape lives, Peter. At last year's conference, a cadet from Texas A&M called the experience of lunch with WWII heroes "truly inspirational -- something I will never forget." At nearly every meal, young cadets will sit side-by-side with World War II heroes, listening to stories of Normandy, Iwo Jima, and the Chosin Reservoir - thanks to you. At the evening reception, post-9/11 veterans will raise a glass in fellowship - thanks to you, Peter. These simple moments carry a profound message: America remembers, honors, and thanks her veterans - and inspires the next generation to follow in their footsteps. Peter, A small gift from you creates a big memory for both sides. When a cadet hears about D-Day directly from the man who landed there, history becomes real. When a Vietnam hero raises a glass at our reception this Friday, he knows he's not forgotten. These small acts of generosity turn into powerful memories...meals become conversations...beers become toasts. And veterans realize America still remembers. Thanks to you, Peter, our heroes will not only share their stories but will know their service is still remembered and celebrated -- more than 80 years after many of them first answered the call. Tim Holbert President American Veterans Center P.S. Peter, the simplest gift -- a meal, a beer -- can leave the deepest impression on America's veterans. The mission of the American Veterans Center and the World War II Veterans Committee is to preserve and promote the legacy and experiences of America's veterans and active duty service personnel from World War II through today. A non-profit educational organization, the AVC and WWIIVC are funded solely through generous contributions from people like you. All contributions are tax-deductible. body:

#
Friend, it’s no secret that Republicans have made Kristen and MI-08 one of their top targets for the upcoming 2026 election. They’ve already spent almost **$1 million** on attack ads against Kristen -- even though the election is still a year away. **Now they’re back at it again. ** A GOP-linked super PAC just launched another deceptive ad campaign, distorting Kristen’s record in a desperate attempt to protect their fragile House majority. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118948769/581425621/-185804085?refcode=em-11.09.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjAyNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYTExYjg2Mi03NGJkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TLNP61t95uA1Zcae8xN0oecQuQoFY2M6PkbasYPtM0U=) Here’s the truth: Kristen is working relentlessly to lower health care costs, create good-paying jobs, and make our communities safer. name: Alexandra Rojas (via JusticeDemocrats.com)
subject: re: Zohran Mamdani
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

In case you missed it, Peter:

Last Tuesday, Democratic socialist Zohran Mamdani won his race against a
corporate, out-of-touch political dynasty to become the next mayor of New
York City.

This is huge.

Zohran’s victory is the turning point that our progressive movement has
been working toward for years within the Democratic Party: electing
leaders with the moral courage to unite voters to take on Republican
authoritarianism, Democratic corporatism, and billionaire greed all at
once.

It proves what Justice Democrats has been saying since our founding:
Democratic primaries are the battleground for the control of our party’s
direction.

We can send leaders like Zohran — who share the same vision of improving
everyday folks’ lives by making the wealthiest pay their fair share — to
fight for us in Congress, and it starts with next year’s Democratic
primaries.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251109.acAZAE&refcode=em20251109-37175&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1002&akid=37175%2E1203867%2EFOJgbm ]Will you help us build our progressive, working-class movement and
elect more Zohrans to office in 2026 by pitching in a grassroots
contribution of $5 or more now?

For too long, wealthy donors and billionaire lobbies have demanded
cowardice and moral vagueness from our politicians. As a result,
Democratic voters have had entrenched corporate politicians like Andrew
Cuomo stuffed down their throats as the only possible alternative to
Republican representation.

But working-class voters are waking up to realize they are represented by
a party unwilling to stand up to the biggest bullies and robber barons of
our time — and they’re walking away. This is the failure of Party
leadership, and it is in large part why Democrats lost so badly last
November.

What party pundits and strategists should take away from Zohran’s win is
that voters want leaders who have the moral clarity to stand up for their
most urgent needs and are brave enough to take on the powerful
billionaires, lobbies, and corporations who are standing in the way of
their prosperity.

It is because Zohran took on the real estate corporations, the Israel
lobby, and the billionaires robbing everyone blind that voters saw a
candidate they could actually believe would fight for them — not in spite
of it.

His victory builds on the wins from members of Congress like Summer Lee,
AOC, Rashida Tlaib, Delia Ramirez, Ilhan Omar, and others who finally
offered Democratic voters an alternative choice to the same corporate,
career politicians that have ruled the Party for decades.

Zohran’s win provides a clear mandate: our party can and must embrace
moral courage as a deliberate political strategy. If it does, we won’t
just move the conversation — we'll realign political power itself and put
it back in the hands of the people instead of the millionaires,
billionaires, corporations, and lobby groups.

We cannot let this victory and momentum end in NYC,
Peter. It must be a signal to political
candidates across the country that we CAN take on the wealthiest, most
well-connected, most entrenched corporate politicians and win, if they’re
willing to be a voice that unites the working class against the wealthy
elites.

Now is the moment to build on our work to transform the Democratic Party
through Congressional primary elections nationwide. Justice Democrats has
been the leading organization waging a war for the future of our party in
primaries since 2017, and this cycle, we’re planning on taking on as many
do-nothing, corporate Democrats as possible.

The next electoral fight is the 2026 midterms, and at a time when the
Party has some of its lowest approvals ever, the only way for us to take
power back from a Republican trifecta is to run candidates that people
actually can believe in, see themselves in, and be inspired by. Candidates
like Donavan McKinney, Angela Gonzales-Torres, Justin J. Pearson, Cori
Bush, and more to come soon.

The era of Cuomo and Schumer, who have controlled Democratic politics for
decades, is over. Voters want new leaders, with ideas bold enough to match
the scale of their crises, and the path to electing them is in Democratic
primaries across the country, where Justice Democrats is leading the
charge.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251109.acAZAE&refcode=em20251109-37175&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1003&akid=37175%2E1203867%2EFOJgbm ]But just like it took a whole movement to defeat Andrew Cuomo, it's
going to take a groundswell of grassroots resources to defeat the rest of
the billionaire-backed politicians in the Democratic Party. Let’s multiply
this victory and keep building an unstoppable movement: chip in whatever
you can today to directly fuel our work this cycle.

clean_body: [1]Justice Democrats In case you missed it, Peter: Last Tuesday, Democratic socialist Zohran Mamdani won his race against a corporate, out-of-touch political dynasty to become the next mayor of New York City. This is huge. Zohran’s victory is the turning point that our progressive movement has been working toward for years within the Democratic Party: electing leaders with the moral courage to unite voters to take on Republican authoritarianism, Democratic corporatism, and billionaire greed all at once. It proves what Justice Democrats has been saying since our founding: Democratic primaries are the battleground for the control of our party’s direction. We can send leaders like Zohran — who share the same vision of improving everyday folks’ lives by making the wealthiest pay their fair share — to fight for us in Congress, and it starts with next year’s Democratic primaries. [ ]Will you help us build our progressive, working-class movement and elect more Zohrans to office in 2026 by pitching in a grassroots contribution of $5 or more now? For too long, wealthy donors and billionaire lobbies have demanded cowardice and moral vagueness from our politicians. As a result, Democratic voters have had entrenched corporate politicians like Andrew Cuomo stuffed down their throats as the only possible alternative to Republican representation. But working-class voters are waking up to realize they are represented by a party unwilling to stand up to the biggest bullies and robber barons of our time — and they’re walking away. This is the failure of Party leadership, and it is in large part why Democrats lost so badly last November. What party pundits and strategists should take away from Zohran’s win is that voters want leaders who have the moral clarity to stand up for their most urgent needs and are brave enough to take on the powerful billionaires, lobbies, and corporations who are standing in the way of their prosperity. It is because Zohran took on the real estate corporations, the Israel lobby, and the billionaires robbing everyone blind that voters saw a candidate they could actually believe would fight for them — not in spite of it. His victory builds on the wins from members of Congress like Summer Lee, AOC, Rashida Tlaib, Delia Ramirez, Ilhan Omar, and others who finally offered Democratic voters an alternative choice to the same corporate, career politicians that have ruled the Party for decades. Zohran’s win provides a clear mandate: our party can and must embrace moral courage as a deliberate political strategy. If it does, we won’t just move the conversation — we'll realign political power itself and put it back in the hands of the people instead of the millionaires, billionaires, corporations, and lobby groups. We cannot let this victory and momentum end in NYC, Peter. It must be a signal to political candidates across the country that we CAN take on the wealthiest, most well-connected, most entrenched corporate politicians and win, if they’re willing to be a voice that unites the working class against the wealthy elites. Now is the moment to build on our work to transform the Democratic Party through Congressional primary elections nationwide. Justice Democrats has been the leading organization waging a war for the future of our party in primaries since 2017, and this cycle, we’re planning on taking on as many do-nothing, corporate Democrats as possible. The next electoral fight is the 2026 midterms, and at a time when the Party has some of its lowest approvals ever, the only way for us to take power back from a Republican trifecta is to run candidates that people actually can believe in, see themselves in, and be inspired by. Candidates like Donavan McKinney, Angela Gonzales-Torres, Justin J. Pearson, Cori Bush, and more to come soon. The era of Cuomo and Schumer, who have controlled Democratic politics for decades, is over. Voters want new leaders, with ideas bold enough to match the scale of their crises, and the path to electing them is in Democratic primaries across the country, where Justice Democrats is leading the charge. [ ]But just like it took a whole movement to defeat Andrew Cuomo, it's going to take a groundswell of grassroots resources to defeat the rest of the billionaire-backed politicians in the Democratic Party. Let’s multiply this victory and keep building an unstoppable movement: chip in whatever you can today to directly fuel our work this cycle. [ ]Contribute $5 Our only path to real change, in the face of a full corporate takeover of our country, is to take on the billionaire class head-on and with our full force. Thank you for anything you can do to stand alongside us in this fight. In solidarity, Alexandra Rojas Executive Director Contribute to Justice Democrats:

name: Blake Gendebien
subject: I’m running for the seat Elise Stefanik left behind
disclaimer_text: Paid for by State Democrats Committee (StateDemocrats
body:
I’m Blake Gendebien, the dairy farmer and Democrat running to replace Stefanik and deliver results for rural America.
#
**It’s official: Elise Stefanik is running for governor, and her seat in Congress is wide open.**
I’m Blake Gendebien, the dairy farmer and Democrat running to replace Stefanik and deliver results for rural America.
**[Will you split a $10 donation between my campaign and State Democrats Committee today to become a Founding Donor and help us win this race?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97531?refcode=em20251109-9691&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=10&t=1&akid=9691%2E614464%2Eys4g5x)**
**[DONATE](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97532?refcode=em20251109-9691&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=9691%2E614464%2Eys4g5x)**
Our opponents are going to pour millions of dollars into this race to keep this seat and hold onto their slim majority in Congress, so we have to keep up our momentum. But with less than a year until the election, we don’t have any time to waste.
We’ve already received so much grassroots support, but it’s only a fraction of the amount our opponents are about to flood our district with. Now more than ever, we need to raise even more to spread our message across New York and make sure voters know I’m ready to fight for them in Congress.
**[I know we can win this if we work together. Will you split a $10 donation between my campaign and State Democrats Committee today to join our team?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97531?refcode=em20251109-9691&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=10&t=3&akid=9691%2E614464%2Eys4g5x)**
If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly:
[Contribute $5](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97531?refcode=em20251109-9691&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=5&t=4&akid=9691%2E614464%2Eys4g5x) [Contribute $15](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97531?refcode=em20251109-9691&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=15&t=5&akid=9691%2E614464%2Eys4g5x) [Contribute $30](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97531?refcode=em20251109-9691&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=30&t=6&akid=9691%2E614464%2Eys4g5x) [Contribute $50](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97531?refcode=em20251109-9691&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=50&t=7&akid=9691%2E614464%2Eys4g5x) [Contribute $100](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97531?refcode=em20251109-9691&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=100&t=8&akid=9691%2E614464%2Eys4g5x) [Contribute $250](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97531?refcode=em20251109-9691&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=250&t=9&akid=9691%2E614464%2Eys4g5x) [Give another amount](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97532?refcode=em20251109-9691&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=10&akid=9691%2E614464%2Eys4g5x)

Blake

name: Charlotte Bergmann
subject: REMINDER: You are Invited to Tuesday's Veterans Day Kickoff Meeting
body:
View this email in your browser (https://mailchi.mp/4667ea7594ef/meeting-5858215?e=9dd54a4530)
SAVE THE DATE
Veterans Day Gathering
Tuesday, November 11, 2025, 3:00 PM
Perkins Restaurant, 1340 Germantown Parkway

------------------------------------------------------------

** Join us
------------------------------------------------------------
Peter, join fellow Memphians exploring new options for congressional representation in Tennessee’s 9th District. This Veterans Day we’ll gather to celebrate service, share community priorities, and discuss a path forward that puts families and neighborhood progress first.
------------------------------------------------------------

** What to expect
------------------------------------------------------------

* A presentation from Candidate Charlotte Bergmann on her vision for the 9th District, why she aligned with Republican principles long before MAGA, and how those principles can be used to lift ambassadors and their communities.
* A conversation about sustaining the momentum created by the Memphis Safe Task Force work and translating those wins into lasting investments for Memphis.
* Time for questions, community priorities, and discussion of concrete changes Memphians want and deserve.

------------------------------------------------------------

** Why come
------------------------------------------------------------
A safe Memphis is a successful Memphis. When neighborhoods feel secure, businesses invest, visitors return, and families can envision raising their children here with confidence. Come help shape policies and local action that build public safety, attract jobs and investment, and protect the progress already made by community task forces.

**
------------------------------------------------------------
------------------------------------------------------------

** RSVP and contact
------------------------------------------------------------
RSVP to cdoty707choose@gmail.com (mailto:cdoty707choose@gmail.com) . Peter, bring friends, neighbors, and anyone invested in building a stronger future for Memphis.
CRITICAL Campaign Opportunity
Our website hosting, and domains are scheduled to expire on November 30, 2025. We need to raise $610.54 to prevent this from happening. Please consider donating$20, $40, $60 or $100, or the full amount (https://ElectBergmann.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ed4565687fe1f93d61fb32842&id=d746085587&e=9dd54a4530) to keep our website up and running.

Please Donate Here (https://ElectBergmann.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ed4565687fe1f93d61fb32842&id=12a6e6b968&e=9dd54a4530)
Ambassador Badge
------------------------------------------------------------

** Become an Ambassador
------------------------------------------------------------
Sign up at the event to become an ambassador — a committed local leader who will help organize neighbors, spread information, and turn community priorities into action. Ambassadors will receive training, materials, and direct support to make measurable improvements for their families and neighborhoods.

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from YourCapitalMinds.com, an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. name: VoteVets
subject: What happens next
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS, VOTEVETS
body:
We can’t let all of this go to waste. VoteVets is already getting our next campaigns started, and we aren’t going to stop until we’ve taken back the House and the Senate.
#
Peter, we know you can’t donate to every single email VoteVets sends. And we don’t expect you to, either.
Honestly, that would be crazy.
But we do need people to chip in, if they can, from time to time, when it’s important.
This is one of those moments, and we’re hoping you’ll let us explain why.
**FIRST:**
Trump came into office in January ready to destroy our democracy and turn the United States into his own kingdom. Nothing was safe; he did a ton of damage, and people got mad.
**THEN:**
Trump’s approval fell to its lowest point EVER. And when he sat down for dinner on Tuesday, voters served a steaming hot platter of defeats.
**NOW:**
Trump is weak. We have the momentum. And we have to press him relentlessly to keep it that way.
So, what happens next?
We can’t let all of this go to waste. VoteVets is already getting our next campaigns started, and we aren’t going to stop until we’ve taken back the House and the Senate.
So, we’re humbly asking you: **[Chip in $5 so we can keep handing Trump defeats next year.**](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-nov25.xqPykj&refcode=em251109-1-120&t=1&akid=34754%2E3013755%2EaTF0gO)

****
Thank you.
The team at VoteVets

name: Jocelyn Benson
subject: My opponent is spreading dangerous lies about Michigan’s elections
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blue Amp Action
body:
Michigan’s elections are safe and secure, and the results are an accurate reflection of the will of the people. Period.
| | | | |
---
| | | | |
---
| _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T7a6cc4eb-b130-41b7-8071-8d938c0fa7f8/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
| Hey Peter, it’s Jocelyn Benson. One of my opponents in the race for governor recently attacked me — and the voters who rejected him the last time he ran statewide — with dangerous, debunked conspiracy theories about our elections. [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T521df456-74bf-4bbf-99da-6b95c8cace66/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) Let me be clear: Michigan’s elections are safe and secure, and the results are an accurate reflection of the will of the people. Period. That was true in 2020, when John James lost his statewide race — and it’s true today. In fact, the 2020 election was not just safe, secure, and accurate — it was also the most audited election in Michigan’s history. Those results showed clearly that Donald Trump and John James lost. No amount of lies, conjectures, or threats will change that. And now, the fact that James wants to be Michigan’s next governor and is still denying the truth about 2020 is dangerous and disqualifying. And frankly, it’s disappointing. We should all expect more from anyone who wants to lead our state. This kind of rhetoric undermines trust in our elections, threatens the safety of our hardworking election officials, poll workers, and their families, and escalates division and discord at a time when our leaders need to be bringing us together. Peter, our last three statewide elections broke records for turnout. They were transparent, they were safe, and they were secure. Look, I’ve run against sore losers and election deniers before. I can take them on again and win. And as I’ve always done, I will stand up to anyone, anytime, who tries to stand in the way of Michiganders' voices and votes. No political rhetoric or threats will ever deter me from that mission. [That’s why I need your help. Your support today will help me fight back against these attacks, defend our election officials, and protect every Michigander’s right to a fair, secure vote. Will you chip in $10, $25, or whatever you can to stand with me and Blue Amp Action — and stand up for Michigan’s democracy?](https://default.salsalabs.org/T3129f7a0-fae8-4b34-b31e-c3b8412bee16/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [DONATE $10](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tb43611ee-ff68-420e-8f97-8d5dee49608a/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [DONATE $25](https://default.salsalabs.org/T47c95424-a198-4fe9-96a4-dbb3f19b6d04/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [DONATE MORE](https://default.salsalabs.org/T0840be8d-8640-4d3a-92ed-e6fa1132f446/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| Michigan voters deserve a governor who will fight for them and work with everyone to build a future where every family can afford childcare, buy a home in the community they want to live in, and have access to quality education and well-paying jobs. Help me be that governor and deliver for our state. We can do this. Together. Thank you for standing with me. — Jocelyn

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade, it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in a democracy, not a dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tb9bb286d-0540-48cf-8a3b-b8122764398c/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

name: Jon Ossoff for Georgia (via GIFFORDS.org)
subject: Absolutely pummeled
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
#

Team, it’s GIFFORDS. We wanted to share this important update from Senator Jon Ossoff’s team. Jon Ossoff is the most vulnerable Democrat up for election in the country. Read the message below, then [**chip in any amount so we can re-elect him in Georgia.**](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-ossoff-q42025.IqCIRh&refcode=em251109-1-b&t=1&akid=10985%2E3935704%2E1zPfff)
* * *

Friend,
As you read this, Jon Ossoff is getting absolutely pummeled by attack ads in Georgia. Republicans have spent more than _$10 million_ on TV and online already. It’s ridiculous.
Why so much money, this far from Election Day?
Because Jon Ossoff is the only Democratic Senator running for reelection in a state won by Trump. _That_ is why Republicans are hitting him so hard, so early in the campaign.
_And it’s why we’re coming to you with this request to help fight back._
If Jon is going to defend this seat, we really need to keep hitting our fundraising goals, and donations to emails like this are a huge part of how it happens.
[**Can Jon Ossoff count on you to split $5, $50 or whatever you can afford between his campaign and GIFFORDS so we can lay the foundation for a winning Senate reelection campaign in Georgia?**](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-ossoff-q42025.IqCIRh&refcode=em251109-1-b&t=2&akid=10985%2E3935704%2E1zPfff)
**[DONATE](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-ossoff-q42025.IqCIRh&refcode=em251109-1-b&t=3&akid=10985%2E3935704%2E1zPfff)**
We wish we didn’t have to ask at all, but Citizens United opened the dam for limitless dark money to flood into our elections.
_That’s a big part of why Congress is broken and corrupted. It’s why we have really expensive elections._
And also it’s why campaigns like ours have to build serious grassroots fundraising operations _from the very start._
Because if we don’t, we run the risk of our message getting drowned out by a flood of Super PAC spending.
So here’s the truth:
**Jon needs our help today.**
_He is literally MAGA’s #1 target._
They will spare no expense and try every dirty trick in the book.
So yes, this election will be tough.
But this team knows how to win tough fights. We’ve done it before.
Let’s do it again.
[**Please, use this link to split a donation between GIFFORDS and Jon’s reelection campaign. Every donation counts. Every donation sends a powerful message to MAGA that we’re ready to fight and win.**](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-ossoff-q42025.IqCIRh&refcode=em251109-1-b&t=4&akid=10985%2E3935704%2E1zPfff)

Fellow Patriot, They shot my father. Then, they killed my friend Charlie Kirk. And still -- _the Left hasn't won._ But if we back down now... they will. That's why I'm asking you to stand with me -- and support an organization Charlie built to fight back and WIN: **Turning Point Action.** Charlie was assassinated for daring to teach young Americans to love this country. body:
Will you please complete the 2025 Voter Verification Audit AS SOON AS POSSIBLE? |

| **Redistricting push hits roadblocks in Indiana as some lawmakers resist**
— _ABC News_

This is a BIG deal, Peter. Anti-democratic attempts to manipulate maps to unfairly give Republicans more congressional seats have met opposition from some Republican lawmakers in Indiana.
**As of today, Republicans don’t have the votes they need to strip fair representation from _even more_ voters the way they have in states like Texas, Missouri, and North Carolina.**
Since the Trump White House launched a pressure campaign urging GOP lawmakers across the country to gerrymander to rig the midterms, our movement has been fighting back. name: Chris Taylor for Justice
subject: This is the only way we’ll elect Judge Chris Taylor
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Taylor for Justice
body:
# [](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/18?refcode=em20251109-796&t=1&akid=796%2E294397%2EBRGd4u)
Did you see the news out of Pennsylvania this week?
Voters there just beat back the right-wing dark money groups trying to buy seats on their state Supreme Court — proving that when people come together, grassroots power _wins._
**But now that those same dark money groups are done with Pennsylvania, they could turn their attention to Wisconsin.**
Next April, voters will decide who sits on Wisconsin’s highest court — and whether the Wisconsin Supreme Court continues to stand up for fairness, democracy, and the rights of all people.
The only way we’ll elect Judge Chris Taylor and defeat her dark money-backed opponent is by generating enough grassroots momentum to reach voters and make sure they know what’s at stake.
That’s why we’re asking: [**Can you pitch in $10 to help us prepare for whatever the far-right money machine throws at us?**](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/31?c0=em_ms_jct_20251109_1_ak.HACxpM&amount=10&refcode=em20251109-796&t=3&akid=796%2E294397%2EBRGd4u)

Thank you,
Team Taylor

name: Mark Kelly
subject: It hasn’t been easy
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
But with a good team, a lot of hard work, and a lot more luck, I made it this far.
#

Folks —
Sometimes I wonder what this guy would think about where he ended up:
[](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-nov25/.mVeyuq&refcode=em251109-1-8k&t=1&akid=6240%2E6688874%2E9A7Cpo)
He had no idea what the next few decades would look like, and he had a whole lot of growing up to do.
It wasn’t easy. It’s not easy dodging surface-to-air missiles over Iraq. It’s not easy flying a rocket ship. It’s not easy when the woman you love is shot. And it hasn’t been easy seeing the richest guy on earth and the President of the United States attack our democracy and drag my name through the mud.
So, yeah, it wasn’t easy. But with a good team, a lot of hard work, and a lot more luck, I made it this far. Much of that is thanks to you and I really can’t tell you how grateful I am that you’ve stuck with me all this time.
And honestly, it shouldn’t take more than that. But I’ve got one more request for you.
[**Can I count on you for a donation before midnight tonight? It adds up fast when a lot of people are chipping in, and I’d really appreciate your support.**](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-nov25/.mVeyuq&refcode=em251109-1-8k&t=2&akid=6240%2E6688874%2E9A7Cpo)

name: Former Navy SEAL Adam Schwarze
subject: Good luck, New York
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Schwarze for Senate
body:
As a former Navy SEAL who has served the United States in more than 70 countries around the globe, I’m gobsmacked | [](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=bcf9202e2d2d41bab070838c0398352f&_e=BRrCXHFt5ZZ993xhyu6dLzd4AcSpchxTJHb_u9P0cCaTkXGR6153zQes2ACQpbgbPniiiKgAxvhGqZfg7RSCHZiKFtzPdDF4nOqHrzMs4ly2FowIyuy7OtkZRFaMElH8Rz8BrBiyISZrDgtYkJPcXsoRwjuNbUM063gc2H06eWnXsJAk16ySGCou-hCedwDpfqnEor2ALmPkqL38eA30m88nS8aeL8ZW4V4Aopu9gznflEYukGyyik5H6XlaaRvotVv96XlericOFMHguLfqLZumHXXBPDGZ59fDbZPiee31k6iAQCFKwow5XsMuUxye77OjS9G3u3yTnT5VEw9irN9SJO5H0Kzk71-9NrE86VreLlpurfNOjRUIIxwM7bgqAi9l9nVvvvFzvpk-t93iOCB0o8C_SOzdcHPfscrsbyHypuSTjxe76sIVeLkxEoypkb1j3O8VjphAScnnEPwd49RTO3m99AJEADyi8UVmwdVYyV-k3NHxLYJwOdtdxQx84o72r2T58lYyp-r1NMev86NFSpYBCk90TkKC3wNQu1cgmix1ldK2EJQaJBczE_j818MBXdNNO7XBnQbDZIT42HeLUVL8_0x8irChzj6-osfF-ismZe222iz3hXxV7077XBLODPPW6bvmJZ_Sm0a9ffGehkVJH53IRet0jJC_X2gUwgxkEVKuYt2jjpBABJVIgXRrqt3B0zOCIY0UwTUrGFIxzu0AtWwOnragNg6uxAbdfLb59t5kkYhkMoVhdL4eMQ-tj748XI-VBtXDiaTbtdFMUZVfjB8aBYRnw6B3_cM%3D)

---
Fellow Conservative,

As a former Navy SEAL who has served our nation in 70 foreign countries, I’m gobsmacked by the news out of New York.

**I’m thinking of all the lives we’ve lost against this enemy over the last century, and I want to weep.**

clean_body: | | | Trump is selling favors for his ballroom? Trump’s latest insult to the American people? The Ballroom of Corruption – a gilded playground where billionaire donors will dance the night away after cutting checks to the “felon-emperor” himself. A of the “guests” funding Trump’s ballroom recently faced federal enforcement actions. Nothing says “drain the swamp” like polluters, fraudsters, and grifters who owe the government millions funding Trump’s northern MaraLago. Trump’s turned the people’s presidency into an open bar for bad actors, and we’re done watching him waltz away with our democracy. That’s why we’ve launched a billboard campaign across America to IMPEACH TRUMP NOW – and remind everyone that Trump is a crooked conman and creep UNFIT for office. We’ve already got more than a dozen billboards roadside, our most recent in Memphis, Tennessee, but we need your help to expand the campaign and pile on the pressure. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. ** Thanks for fueling the fight, -Cedar Key Progress | Cedar Key Progress support Democrats in competitive Southern districts who are building a diverse, inclusive, and just future. name: James Carville
subject: I'm fuming.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Pappas for Senate
body:
If we don’t get off our butts and help Chris Pappas right now, we can kiss the Senate majority goodbye.
# [](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/126?t=1&akid=3374%2E582294%2E8ZAl2h)
Folks, it’s James Carville.
I’ve been around the block a few times and worked on a heck of a lot of campaigns. So believe me when I tell you: **This ain’t good.**
John Sununu — yeah, _those Sununus_ — jumped into the U.S. Senate race in New Hampshire. And now the political eggheads have DOWNGRADED Democrats’ chances of winning there.
**[💥 Before you read another word, chip in $10 or whatever you can to help Chris Pappas fight back right now.](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/221?c0=em_ms_cp_20251109_1_carville.MyZLbV&refcode=em20251109-1&t=2&akid=3374%2E582294%2E8ZAl2h)**
Let me tell you what this means: **If we don’t get off our butts and help Chris Pappas right now, we can kiss our chances of winning back the Senate majority goodbye.**
This isn’t some back-bench, no-name guy we’re up against. **Sununu’s won a Senate seat before.**
He’s got name ID, deep pockets, and Wall Street’s dark money machine ready to drown us in attack ads. This is the far-right’s big play, y’all — they know if they take New Hampshire, Democrats have _no viable path_ to flipping the Senate.
Chris Pappas is the real deal. I mean it. He’s won the toughest district in New Hampshire four damn times, and he knows how to bring folks together. But he cannot — **_cannot_** — do this alone.
So here’s what I need from you:
**[Rush a contribution to Chris Pappas today. $10, $25, whatever you can spare. Because if we let the GOP buy this seat for Sununu, we’re gonna be stuck with Republican control for years to come.](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/221?c0=em_ms_cp_20251109_1_carville.MyZLbV&refcode=em20251109-1&t=3&akid=3374%2E582294%2E8ZAl2h)**
**[Chip in Now »](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251109_1_carville.8nseOk&refcode=em20251109-1&t=4&akid=3374%2E582294%2E8ZAl2h)**
I’m mad as HELL that this race just shifted in the wrong direction — but that means it’s time to get serious. Chris is the only one who can hold this seat, but he needs a hell of a lot more firepower to fend off what’s coming.
Let’s show these Republican bigshots that we’re not scared — and we sure as hell ain’t backing down.
**[Give what you can right now.](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/221?c0=em_ms_cp_20251109_1_carville.MyZLbV&refcode=em20251109-1&t=5&akid=3374%2E582294%2E8ZAl2h)** Let’s win this damn thing and flip control of the Senate.
— James Carville
[](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/221?c0=em_ms_cp_20251109_1_carville.MyZLbV&refcode=em20251109-1&t=6&akid=3374%2E582294%2E8ZAl2h)
**New Hampshire is poised to be one of the closest Senate races in the country in 2026. Will you chip in today to help Chris stop the Republicans from flipping the Granite State red and expanding their Senate majority?**
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_cp_footer?refcode=em20251109-1&t=7&refcode2=3374_582294_8ZAl2h&akid=3374%2E582294%2E8ZAl2h)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.chrispappas.org/signup/cps_receive_fewer_emails/?t=8&akid=3374%2E582294%2E8ZAl2h)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.chrispappas.org/signup/cps_update_your_information/?t=9&akid=3374%2E582294%2E8ZAl2h)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.chrispappas.org/unsubscribe/cps_unsubscribe/?t=10&akid=3374%2E582294%2E8ZAl2h)

name: Democrats.org
subject: Quickly explaining what’s next →
body:
---
**An update for Democrats nationwide** Derek, please don’t skip this 1-minute read. **This week, Democrats in every corner of the country won or overperformed up and down the ballot, with high voter turnout across the map.** If one thing is crystal clear this week, it’s that voters are sick and tired of Trump and his allies’ billionaire-first agenda and are ready for the tides to turn. In New Jersey, Virginia, Pennsylvania, California, Georgia, and in local races nationwide, Americans sent a message to Trump this week: _We’re fed up with your attacks on our democracy, our economy, and our communities.**We’re fighting back. And we’re winning.**_ At the DNC, we’ve spent the last year organizing in every single state across the country, hosting town halls where Republicans refuse to, and investing in state Democratic parties to ensure that they have the resources to do this important work across their states. **But Derek, the work doesn’t stop here** — in fact, our fight is only getting started. This week, Democrats in every corner of the country won or overperformed up and down the ballot, with high voter turnout across the map. If one thing is crystal clear this week, it’s that voters are sick and tired of Trump and his allies’ billionaire-first agenda and are ready for the tides to turn. disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress
body:
He eliminated $700 million in medical debt. Imagine what he can do as a U.S. Senator. | | | [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.X6XUC7u8ppFLNtMO_55SZlNseb7gEYIOhm_EWnhzEOZKmTtnV3k2h4V2x_O3H7rdW2az5rgktBwzxQ8Ml9Wgkmv85qW-UsBonL4KRa0bcNqdICU6T3NBDdNIZG7w7wEWxfFzlBClFCHRsD1UGe0DtPB3PoDXTtffaDkDDGpGICvgcrWHO34serDoezf_7h1ZPQ0UAUAAU0epjWW6Dun_23gySaB33TcVybU9lCaCPpXokcjxW0a9qu9gbAM9E0ASKkDzcHD3pFbuqjRZBpHugk-wfvUm7-f4tU7UoMPr9H6VHExuAoBwcW2vmsrerd3wJFYYmDnRgVcnb2rg9R9469gXM211p-vpSIyYKyzSbTQtW9Du5mbhXnIsqL8CTSeryOxDOnPiFxaU1XnqqxU_SH_QZw7xaZp84cxBSBDJEXT2-jdoVrzX6KI73P7umoWZ0zIg1VZvopipLwuOpcv3cg/4lg/1dPO5JCvSk25CtY1V9H10A/h0/h001.J61gaZZyN-b6qRjopKahGdCm_Uyc4SE7Dwtnq8zg3PE)

Friend, I’m endorsing a true progressive for Michigan’s open U.S. Senate seat: **Dr. Abdul El-Sayed**. **Abdul is the only candidate in the race who hasn’t pandered to AIPAC and who’s fighting for transformative changes like Medicare for All and ending medical debt.** He’s already cancelled over $700 million in medical debt for Michigan families, removed lead from Detroit’s elementary schools, secured free glasses for tens of thousands of kids, and made life-saving Narcan universally accessible. Imagine what he’ll do in the U.S. Senate. **This will be one of the most competitive races in the country, and Abdul is up against massive spending by AIPAC and corporate PACs.** But polling shows Abdul is just two points behind AIPAC’s preferred candidate. With grassroots support, we can win this competitive primary and the general election. [**Can you donate to our campaigns today to keep building the bench of progressive Democrats and showing that people power can overcome big money in politics?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsXu0R1XDWRVJnEMC9_ePsJbLF0No64CI-SVkzJypM18cEpS14HbBNaOrp1fIcDkN2xSjYpSaqoNeYrBZgQNZA1tSD43CU0_XdeCZTT_6myJqdAKB5YiZYxIxZwl-64uLwoResGJzbo11AeEhoiZavRjOebHep1uhC2hg7mhzgGnQe1Wu8Mz4yuRqcHTNgaMddG0vxc-tPT6dkayz_X3i73Q4H4Y2nim3OOUQRK4V1KYcw0xLncjn9jHr88obJ0vTEykFegWeXHvGmz6Q3WT87y9vyLV8GiTacJfwrMDhVWiiaOOORBlSzdiCtjSMSBEk_po75CNb8LLtWUbILNFZwy_d3_LNv0JmjQPvxF3oggpoYlyza_Us4ERgHA133SCmSin7L-NjLxyAfsa0dVH41HnmXNUFLglElGiDSFc9Od3MtcqOWDcee89yeJysk2OGpNwVvEm2jpQVhNUtp5UnRtaS1GqIIH22w-HP9Vz7ChkzV7B3ac71CNZqHSFD_eLkU/4lg/1dPO5JCvSk25CtY1V9H10A/h1/h001.mmLwUe4RVPVn88xQaWNN5KbtSD5BtXbamAVrokh_wuo) We’ve seen established Democratic Party leadership spread anti-Muslim attacks and refuse to endorse Zohran Mamdani, or say he’s “not the future of the Democratic party.” But **progressives like Zohran and Abdul are not only the future, they’re urgently needed in public office**. They’re _actually_ aligned with what the American people want and need, and their boldness inspires voters. Like me, Zohran, and most Americans, Abdul is demanding U.S. tax dollars go to struggling American communities rather than war crimes. Both Muslim and Arab American—historically underrepresented communities—Abdul and Zohran understand that **we can’t simply tinker with the unjust status quo. We have to actually make things better for all Americans** , not just those at the top. While billionaires hoard more wealth, more Americans are living in poverty, and most already live paycheck to paycheck. We need transformative change, now. [**Let’s elect the first Arab American, Muslim U.S. Senator—who fights for the people rather than billionaires or corporations. Understandably, the American people are disappointed by corporate Democrats who’ve failed to deliver what the people need, often prioritizing corporate donors like weapons companies over their constituents’ lives.
We need Democratic leaders who represent our voices, not those who pander to billionaire-funded Super PACs. Too many Americans have been swayed by racist fearmongering, which billionaires use to distract us from their growing wealth and power.
Too many Democratic political leaders think we should keep moving further to the right, abandoning vulnerable Americans. But that’s not only wrong, it’s unstrategic and ineffective.
**The Democratic Party should be the party of, by, and for marginalized and working people—not a party that compromises with racists or fascists.**
We inspire voters by listening to our base and by demanding the bold changes that people need to survive and thrive. That’s how Abdul can win this Senate seat—as long as he has the grassroots funds to connect with as many voters as possible. Every donation makes a big difference.
[**Please join me in supporting this progressive, Muslim, Arab American fighter for the people. clean_body: He eliminated $700 million in medical debt. Imagine what he can do as a U.S. Senator. | | | Friend, I’m endorsing a true progressive for Michigan’s open U.S. Senate seat: Dr. Abdul El-Sayed. Abdul is the only candidate in the race who hasn’t pandered to AIPAC and who’s fighting for transformative changes like Medicare for All and ending medical debt. He’s already cancelled over $700 million in medical debt for Michigan families, removed lead from Detroit’s elementary schools, secured free glasses for tens of thousands of kids, and made life-saving Narcan universally accessible. Imagine what he’ll do in the U.S. Senate. This will be one of the most competitive races in the country, and Abdul is up against massive spending by AIPAC and corporate PACs. But polling shows Abdul is just two points behind AIPAC’s preferred candidate. With grassroots support, we can win this competitive primary and the general election. We’ve seen established Democratic Party leadership spread anti-Muslim attacks and refuse to endorse Zohran Mamdani, or say he’s “not the future of the Democratic party.” But progressives like Zohran and Abdul are not only the future, they’re urgently needed in public office. They’re actually aligned with what the American people want and need, and their boldness inspires voters. Like me, Zohran, and most Americans, Abdul is demanding U.S. tax dollars go to struggling American communities rather than war crimes. Both Muslim and Arab American—historically underrepresented communities—Abdul and Zohran understand that we can’t simply tinker with the unjust status quo. We have to actually make things better for all Americans , not just those at the top. While billionaires hoard more wealth, more Americans are living in poverty, and most already live paycheck to paycheck. We need transformative change, now. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately, split evenly between Abdul El-Sayed and Rashida Tlaib | | | | | | | | | | Here’s a photo from when I joined Abdul on a panel about how we’ve taken on corporate polluters and helped secure the right to clean air and water: Understandably, the American people are disappointed by corporate Democrats who’ve failed to deliver what the people need, often prioritizing corporate donors like weapons companies over their constituents’ lives. We need Democratic leaders who represent our voices, not those who pander to billionaire-funded Super PACs. Too many Americans have been swayed by racist fearmongering, which billionaires use to distract us from their growing wealth and power. Too many Democratic political leaders think we should keep moving further to the right, abandoning vulnerable Americans. But that’s not only wrong, it’s unstrategic and ineffective. The Democratic Party should be the party of, by, and for marginalized and working people—not a party that compromises with racists or fascists. We inspire voters by listening to our base and by demanding the bold changes that people need to survive and thrive. That’s how Abdul can win this Senate seat—as long as he has the grassroots funds to connect with as many voters as possible. Every donation makes a big difference. Thank you for fighting for a government that works for the people, not corporations. Abdul isn’t stuck in the limited imagination that plagues so many of our leaders. He’s directly connecting with the people and changing the conversation about what’s possible. This is the leadership we need and deserve. Additionally, polling shows Abdul is only two points behind AIPAC’s preferred candidate. With grassroots support, we can win this competitive primary and the general election. Friend, together we’ll create a better future where everyone can thrive. In solidarity, Rashida \---------- Forwarded message --------- From: Rashida Tlaib** Date: Sat, Nov 8, 2025 Subject: Abdul’s the only candidate for Medicare for All and ending medical debt in his race for Senate To: dpwillis67@gmail.com Friend, I’m proud to endorse Dr. Abdul El-Sayed for Michigan’s open U.S. Senate seat. He’s running to build a government that actually works for working people, not corporations or would-be oligarchs. Abdul is the only candidate in the race who’s never taken a dime from corporate PACs, and who has never pandered to pro genocide groups like AIPAC. While Abdul will focus on protecting human rights, he’s running against one of the nation’s top recipients of AIPAC donations. So he’s up against obscene amounts of money from AIPAC and corporate PACs that are trying to buy another seat in Congress. Abdul is also the only candidate in the race who’s fighting for Medicare for All. He won't waver on access to health care for all. Like me, Abdul is also focused on ending medical debt. No one should be punished for getting sick. As a two-time public health official,he created programs that eliminated $700 million in medical debt for 300,000 people, removed lead from Detroit’s elementary schools, secured free glasses for tens of thousands of kids, and made life-saving Narcan universally accessible. Tonight, I joined his town hall to talk about navigating and fixing our broken healthcare system. Together in Congress, we’ll fight for the bold changes that families need in order to thrive. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately, split evenly between Abdul El-Sayed and Rashida Tlaib | | | | | | | | | Back in 2018, Abdul and I were arrested while protesting with fast food workers to raise the minimum wage and strengthen the right to form unions. Abdul is running because it shouldn’t be this hard in the richest country on Earth to afford groceries, see a doctor, or believe our kids will have it better than us. Abdul and I also share a history of holding corporate polluters accountable and cleaning up our air and water. Here’s us together at a 2023 panel about environmental justice: Born and raised in Michigan, Abdul will be the first Muslim and the first Arab American in the U.S. Senate if he wins. With all the racist, Islamophobic attacks from Democrats and Republicans against Zohran Mamdani, it’s clear we need more Muslims in federal office to represent all Americans—and show that we belong here, including in the halls of power. As we saw with Zohran’s win this week, we can overcome billionaire interference in our democracy if we join together and demand transformative change. Thank you so much. In endorsing Abdul, I’m joining U.S. Senator Bernie Sanders, who will fight alongside Abdul in the Senate for Medicare for All and an arms embargo on Israel. Our fight is only beginning, and there’s so much progress ahead. With you in the fight for better, Rashida * Rashida Tlaib for Congress PO Box 32777 Detroit, MI 48232 | Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress | | Sent via . name: Chrissy Houlahan
subject: I confronted Mike Johnson on the Capitol steps
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chrissy Houlahan for Congress
body:
I couldn't stay silent while the Speaker deflected blame for the longest shutdown in history.
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118966969/581481609/-1072566757?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NDkxMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYjlhZmIyMi04NWJkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=OmySEVBucoxZcugrlVnCBlLde8ww250_ZgTm67sQJMI=&emci=4b5882b3-04bc-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&emdi=fb9afb22-85bd-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ceid=21278485)
---
Derek –

I just confronted Speaker Johnson on the U.S. Capitol steps. Why? I couldn't stay silent while he deflected blame for the longest government shutdown in our nation’s history. I spoke up and demanded accountability.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118966982/581481613/685235109?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk1189-fr-nd&recurring=0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NDkxMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYjlhZmIyMi04NWJkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=OmySEVBucoxZcugrlVnCBlLde8ww250_ZgTm67sQJMI=)
Enough is enough. Enough political posturing. Enough chaos. Enough of President Trump and his enablers in Congress refusing to meet with Democrats, refusing to end the needless suffering, refusing to put people over politics.
I said to Speaker Johnson: **“You have an obligation to call the leadership of both parties and bring us together and solve this problem together.”**
His response? He tried to blame Democrats and lecture me but I didn't back down. We, all of us, are sick and tired of being held hostage by extreme politics, and I was there to remind him that his job is to govern – not to grandstand.
This moment is about more than just a confrontation on a set of steps. It is a snapshot of exactly why I ran for Congress: _to be a leader who fights for common sense and bipartisanship, not to join a party that values political victory over delivering results._
I’ve been trying to work across the aisle on paid family leave, on supporting our veterans, and on addressing the real needs of our farmers – but all of that vital work is put on hold when the Speaker chooses chaos and shutdowns.
The other side is seeing our momentum, especially after this week’s election results, and they know our moderate, problem-solving approach is a threat to their extreme agenda.
**[I cannot stand up to the extreme fringe alone. I need you with me today. Will you chip in $5, $25, or whatever you can afford right now to keep me fighting for our values in Congress?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118966986/581481628/685235109?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk1189-fr-nd&recurring=0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NDkxMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYjlhZmIyMi04NWJkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=OmySEVBucoxZcugrlVnCBlLde8ww250_ZgTm67sQJMI=)**

clean_body: I couldn't stay silent while the Speaker deflected blame for the longest shutdown in history. | | | Derek – I just confronted Speaker Johnson on the U.S. Capitol steps. Why? I couldn't stay silent while he deflected blame for the longest government shutdown in our nation’s history. I spoke up and demanded accountability. = Enough is enough. Enough political posturing. Enough chaos. Enough of President Trump and his enablers in Congress refusing to meet with Democrats, refusing to end the needless suffering, refusing to put people over politics. I said to Speaker Johnson: “You have an obligation to call the leadership of both parties and bring us together and solve this problem together.” His response? He tried to blame Democrats and lecture me but I didn't back down. We, all of us, are sick and tired of being held hostage by extreme politics, and I was there to remind him that his job is to govern – not to grandstand. This moment is about more than just a confrontation on a set of steps. It is a snapshot of exactly why I ran for Congress: to be a leader who fights for common sense and bipartisanship, not to join a party that values political victory over delivering results. I’ve been trying to work across the aisle on paid family leave, on supporting our veterans, and on addressing the real needs of our farmers – but all of that vital work is put on hold when the Speaker chooses chaos and shutdowns. The other side is seeing our momentum, especially after this week’s election results, and they know our moderate, problem-solving approach is a threat to their extreme agenda. = If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | | = | | = | | = Thank you for being in this fight with me. That's why I'm asking you to stand with me -- and support an organization Charlie built to fight back and WIN: **Turning Point Action.** Charlie was assassinated for daring to teach young Americans to love this country. My father was almost murdered for refusing to give up on America. **My new book -- _Under Siege_ -- is the story of how we've endured and how YOU can help fight back.**

**PREVIOUS EMAIL:** Peter, Last month, the Trump administration said that Supplemental Nutrition Assistance (SNAP) benefits would [_**run out on November 1**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118970375/581491824/-763820790?refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.11.09_SNAPFollowup_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMWMxMjBjMy04OGJkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=gffGmi44WYPHsildIVOzoVy5WW84dbtuNchfoQgrZ40=&emci=b7f30e80-7dbd-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&emdi=01c120c3-88bd-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ceid=23483442). Today, they announced they will [_**partially fund SNAP**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118970376/581491825/-1902967289?id=127138175&refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.11.09_SNAPFollowup_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMWMxMjBjMy04OGJkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=gffGmi44WYPHsildIVOzoVy5WW84dbtuNchfoQgrZ40=&emci=b7f30e80-7dbd-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&emdi=01c120c3-88bd-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ceid=23483442), but the process could take months. **While Emily and House Democrats work toward a solution in Congress, ALL of us can step up to help the 115,000 of our neighbors in Washington’s 6th District who receive SNAP benefits (also known in WA as Basic Food):** **• Donate –** This is the most helpful thing you can do to address immediate needs. Use this tool from [_**211 Washington**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118970377/581491826/1606638702?query=BD-1800.2000&query_label=Food+Pantries%2FBanks&query_type=taxonomy&refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.11.09_SNAPFollowup_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMWMxMjBjMy04OGJkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=gffGmi44WYPHsildIVOzoVy5WW84dbtuNchfoQgrZ40=&emci=b7f30e80-7dbd-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&emdi=01c120c3-88bd-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ceid=23483442) to find your nearest pantry or food bank. If you’re in the Bremerton area, you can support [_**Bremerton Foodline.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118970378/581491827/-1936493732?refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.11.09_SNAPFollowup_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMWMxMjBjMy04OGJkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=gffGmi44WYPHsildIVOzoVy5WW84dbtuNchfoQgrZ40=&emci=b7f30e80-7dbd-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&emdi=01c120c3-88bd-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ceid=23483442) While donations of non-perishable food are always appreciated, donating money allows local food banks to purchase exactly what their community needs. Since they buy in bulk, $1 donated to a food bank can buy as much as $11 worth of groceries. **• Volunteer –** If you can give your time, you can do many different things: Host a food drive, sort and pack donations, deliver meals, help people apply for benefits, and more. [_**Northwest Harvest**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118970379/581491828/-1769395018?refcode=RS_EM_EN_2025.11.09_SNAPFollowup_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMWMxMjBjMy04OGJkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=gffGmi44WYPHsildIVOzoVy5WW84dbtuNchfoQgrZ40=&emci=b7f30e80-7dbd-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&emdi=01c120c3-88bd-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ceid=23483442) has links to volunteer opportunities across the state, and you can always call your local food bank and ask what they need. **• Advocate –** In the short term, we must end the Trump shutdown and fully fund SNAP. clean_body: Thank you to everyone who responded to our previous email about how we can provide food to our neighbors in Washington’s 6th District who need it most! | Peter, Thank you to everyone who responded to our previous email about how we can provide food to our neighbors in Washington’s 6th District who need it most! Here are even MORE local resources to help folks on SNAP ⬇️ • has a map of food pantries, meal sites, and food lockers across Pierce County. • lists dozens of its partner food banks in Grays Harbor, Pacific, Lewis, Thurston, Mason, Jefferson, and Wahkiakum counties. • The Kitsap Food Bank Coalition partners with food pantries and food banks across Kitsap County: • on Bainbridge Island • in Bremerton • in Silverdale • in Poulsbo • in Bremerton • in Kingston • in Port Orchard • in Belfair • The Port Angeles Food Bank and WSU Extension Clallam County created a living document of . While Emily continues to work in D.C. to end the shutdown, restore SNAP funding, and prevent health insurance premiums from skyrocketing, we can ALL play a part in ensuring our neighbors have the food they need. Thank you for everything you do 💙 – the whole team at Emily Randall for Congress PREVIOUS EMAIL: Peter, Last month, the Trump administration said that Supplemental Nutrition Assistance (SNAP) benefits would . Today, they announced they will , but the process could take months. While Emily and House Democrats work toward a solution in Congress, ALL of us can step up to help the 115,000 of our neighbors in Washington’s 6th District who receive SNAP benefits (also known in WA as Basic Food): • Donate – This is the most helpful thing you can do to address immediate needs. Use this tool from to find your nearest pantry or food bank. If you’re in the Bremerton area, you can support While donations of non-perishable food are always appreciated, donating money allows local food banks to purchase exactly what their community needs. Since they buy in bulk, $1 donated to a food bank can buy as much as $11 worth of groceries. • Volunteer – If you can give your time, you can do many different things: Host a food drive, sort and pack donations, deliver meals, help people apply for benefits, and more. has links to volunteer opportunities across the state, and you can always call your local food bank and ask what they need. • Advocate – In the short term, we must end the Trump shutdown and fully fund SNAP. Here in Washington, the works on these issues year-round. But every issue, from Medicaid and insurance premiums to wages and collective bargaining, is intertwined with hunger. That’s why Team Emily is organizing to elect leaders at the state and federal levels who put people first. Got more suggestions? Reply to this email or reach out on social media. Thank you for being in this fight with us 💙 – the whole team at Emily Randall for Congress | | Emily Randall is proud to represent Washington’s 6th District in Congress. She grew up in a working-class Union household in Port Orchard and was the first in her family to attend college. After serving in the State Senate and passing nearly 200 bills, Emily made history in 2024 as the first queer Latina elected to Congress. Now, Emily is fighting for WA working families in D.C. and organizing to take back the House. name: Team Bohannan
subject: You work harder than Mariannette Miller-Meeks
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CHRISTINA BOHANNAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
In the last 17 weeks, Mariannette Miller-Meeks has only worked a total of 20 days. That's less than most of us work in a single month.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118970647/581492723/-975735282?refcode=em_logoheader&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYzVhNzU3NC04YWJkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SpDbZuNeOXYVsackmNbp6AFFdEaJlzS089vTG874mTg=)

Peter, what if you only showed up to work whenever you wanted to? And even then you _barely_ did the job you were being paid to do?

Working Iowans can't afford to slack off like that, but it's a whole different story for Mariannette Miller-Meeks. **In the last 17 weeks, she has only worked a total of 20 days. That's less than most of us work in a single month.**

While Miller-Meeks is off doing who-knows-what, Iowans are struggling with rising costs, a crumbling healthcare infrastructure, and government dysfunction that's grinding everything to a halt.

**Christina has worked since she was a young teen.** From waiting tables at 14 to cleaning trailers during college, working as an environmental engineer, and teaching classes as a law professor, she knows that to get the job done, the very minimum is showing up.

But Christina has never been about the "bare minimum." That's why, when she gets to Congress, she won't just show up — she'll work every day to gain the trust of the people of our district and champion their voice.

**[But to get her to Washington, we need to fire Mariannette Miller-Meeks. Chip in $5 or whatever you can afford to send Miller-Meeks on a permanent vacation from Congress>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118970648/581492724/-1610961223?refcode=em25_251109_MMWD_fnd_gq_act___TB_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_251109_MMWD_fnd_gq_act___TB_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=cc5a7574-8abd-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYzVhNzU3NC04YWJkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SpDbZuNeOXYVsackmNbp6AFFdEaJlzS089vTG874mTg=)**
