name: JamesTalarico.com
subject: James is statistically tied with Cornyn and Paxton
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Talarico for Texas
body:
#
Peter —
This is huge: the latest public polling shows James in a statistical tie with John Cornyn and Ken Paxton.
That means we’re within striking distance of both of the leading Republican candidates for Texas’ U.S. Senate seat. No matter who they nominate, James can defeat them.
[**Can you help us build on our momentum by making a $5 donation to our campaign today?**](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-launch.bF5RK3&refcode=em251109-1-ak&t=1&akid=216%2E1347274%2E2pbldP)
This is an incredible result, and it shows just how winnable this race is.
Cornyn and Paxton have both won statewide in Texas multiple times. They’ve spent millions on Texans’ screens and have near universal name ID among voters.
The fact that James is neck-and-neck with them despite never running statewide before is a huge testament to the strength of our movement and James’ ability to build a winning coalition in Texas.
But it’s still early. We know that our opponents will be well-funded. Their billionaire mega-donors and right-wing super PACs are going to spend tens of millions of dollars to buy this seat.
So we are counting on people like you to make sure we have the resources to fight back.
[**Please make a donation — $5 or whatever you can spare — to take back Texas and send James Talarico to the U.S. Senate. This race is in the margin of error, and it is going to take all of us to get across the finish line.**](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-launch.bF5RK3&refcode=em251109-1-ak&t=2&akid=216%2E1347274%2E2pbldP)

That means we’re within striking distance of both of the leading Republican candidates for Texas’ U.S. Senate seat. No matter who they nominate, James can defeat them. This is an incredible result, and it shows just how winnable this race is. Cornyn and Paxton have both won statewide in Texas multiple times. They’ve spent millions on Texans’ screens and have near universal name ID among voters. The fact that James is neck-and-neck with them despite never running statewide before is a huge testament to the strength of our movement and James’ ability to build a winning coalition in Texas. But it’s still early. We know that our opponents will be well-funded. Their billionaire mega-donors and right-wing super PACs are going to spend tens of millions of dollars to buy this seat. So we are counting on people like you to make sure we have the resources to fight back. If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly. | | ** | | | | | | | ** Thank you in advance for your support. | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

[ ](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=49db1542a0ba4496aaa1b643433be030&_e=Pn1A8tfJ8COL3NLDjqHXBSJr9YtVyc--4_TPY4b_EbdlaQcgPk_Vwi-V1C-k23mNj3PY3nuWzegkTTCFTseFcGUXaKHhJD1VecIYeF3QwcE3dR5EzqZbqFCApO4pzAC29mohVuviv-pj2EWoqCfQGQ-kU5wUofP5y46zcUlGfrjXgCdk2u4hIwGVXP46ZUrRGbZ2iPVvBvsGbTP9e_4WJnBxtGUSysr17LAL3Ue7M4_Im1N4tAa46Ih1ucyFPFoYzQZEDBx43p3ueM3j874Ar0mlJ4ayPEVqBFWGPSpleJT4el4mJwXaIQPLQJXi_UGW6IbVLXvUSpkJ7XAW3QQiUcq28bJg5QV2UP16EHia0Ejki4tF7bUc0OAVu-_9qHrjMpnU9YeEUD_gw5F1AIZvbbz69_4_2996iZeIpxwG-S_smFoJpaX72tKrDtcVt-l_NCwDClN35REkFg4QR73NcPZfhtJD_iqKN-IhG-ZoD912K-oDtK-PxL7leaiAr9lwNhb7D9dgX9-p8LXKIl_kd3nuIesVKwjufJrjLFvVMDeRe4hEP4eG5n0GJHJE_61w)
One participant—blindfolded and hands free—set out to trial Tesla’s newest AI feature.

No remote. No driver. No contingency.

Then the truck engaged—driven solely by AI.

Each second was authentic, every movement captured.

Engineers and industry observers have been taken aback by the results.

It wasn’t a trick—it was a direct look at the leading edge of automotive autonomy.

Early attention is building around this breakthrough.

[Watch the video that’s prompting conversation across the tech world.](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=49db1542a0ba4496aaa1b643433be030&_e=Pn1A8tfJ8COL3NLDjqHXBSJr9YtVyc--4_TPY4b_EbdlaQcgPk_Vwi-V1C-k23mNj3PY3nuWzegkTTCFTseFcGUXaKHhJD1VecIYeF3QwcE3dR5EzqZbqFCApO4pzAC29mohVuviv-pj2EWoqCfQGQ-kU5wUofP5y46zcUlGfrjXgCdk2u4hIwGVXP46ZUrRS0lH5cVnCHccmE8yuHeHT_xWnJ1f_D5wCDytSTYkk56_uHPY9A9cdVE0R6zTPOQQ_vvCCv5T_I4ZLC4jPGj_AuQhvwv_386sNPnrAvQYw6g0eTmLO5Ef8NjL7bSScS2_Jnt-jAj_w6ft6BWttbp8ZxURW7s_BNt3XQl0cW9lQlb-bOSKOYQPe_QxpY_Ao5KLbrMxi3If9LZCmqdieBYm2ruZrr9KQ08Sb8p31_RG3j-rIzO566CuYD6xVQSjLc6ymMEfvb2kSjE4S5hh2qYKqZvaiOl5pN6lHsPWjmgPacKkBuUZ2gjQufUBhWo3TSSkIJuNnG5c9QIQaVInfInOlF7Jb0YdSufDGyRzaKVtPue9L9ezjP8rlzUpJ8aZxcNs)
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. One participant—blindfolded and hands free—set out to trial Tesla’s newest AI feature. No remote. No driver. No contingency. The team watched and recorded every moment. Then the truck engaged—driven solely by AI. Each second was authentic, every movement captured. Engineers and industry observers have been taken aback by the results. It wasn’t a trick—it was a direct look at the leading edge of automotive autonomy. Early attention is building around this breakthrough. Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from YourCapitalMinds.com, an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. name: Team Rayfield
subject: Fighting to Put Food on Our Tables
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Dan Rayfield
body:
Oregon is keeping the Trump administration in check and delivering on SNAP stamp benefits. | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/danrayfield/countering-trump-tariffs-and-fighting-for-you-20970046?e=1768325bd4)
---
| | | [ ](https://danrayfield.us15.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f0c603f9d9801c198f6fc68fa&id=761638ba0f&e=1768325bd4)
---
|
---
| | | Friend,

When President Trump shut down the federal government, he put the ability to get food on the table at risk for families across the country.

This is frankly un-American. No one deserves to go hungry.

As your Oregon Attorney General, my priority is and always will be to keep you safe, and that includes the ability to feed and take care of your family. **That's why our office, along with Attorneys General across the country, sued the Trump administration to force them to deliver SNAP benefits to our friends and neighbors that need them. **

Friday, after we won in lower courts, Trump appealed to the US Supreme Court just so he wouldn't have to help families in need. We're going to keep fighting. **Here at home, we're not letting Trump make Oregonians go hungry. On Friday, while the temporary restraining order was in place, Oregon ensured our families on food assistance got the help they needed.

Nationally, we're keep our fight going. The rest of Americans need to eat too, and we're not about to let Trump get in the way. **Our lawsuit to force Trump to give food assistance to everyone across the country continues, and we're not about to let him win.

We've got your back, so thanks for being with us. Together, we can make sure our families won't go hungry.

Working for you,
Dan

body:
I have a plan to tackle our housing affordability crisis
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118972323/581499026/-2054079050?refcode=jtk963-surv-nd&code=jtk963-surv-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4OGI3ODkzOC04ZWJkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=n6TlizmP-4raa4HLxerDX3nyzfPb3sXl9dh8HJs9GwI=)
---
If Colorado's going to provide the vision this country needs, friend, then as governor, it's on me to ensure we first meet Coloradans' most basic needs.
I'm talking about the bottom tier of the hierarchy. The must-haves for survival. Food. Water. Air. Housing.
But when rent eats up an entire paycheck (or more) every month, folks don’t have much left to meet other basic needs, let alone have economic security and safety.
**I have a plan to tackle our housing affordability crisis, but I need to make sure it meets the needs of the people I'm running to serve – people like you and 4,999 more members of this grassroots team. _[Can you take a minute or two now to fill out my housing affordability survey before the cutoff at 11:59 p.m.?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118972327/581499034/913257825?refcode=jtk963-surv-nd&code=jtk963-surv-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4OGI3ODkzOC04ZWJkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=n6TlizmP-4raa4HLxerDX3nyzfPb3sXl9dh8HJs9GwI=&emci=3be18d29-b2bc-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&emdi=88b78938-8ebd-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ceid=1295540)_**
| | [Take the Survey](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118972330/581499047/913257825?refcode=jtk963-surv-nd&code=jtk963-surv-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4OGI3ODkzOC04ZWJkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=n6TlizmP-4raa4HLxerDX3nyzfPb3sXl9dh8HJs9GwI=&emci=3be18d29-b2bc-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&emdi=88b78938-8ebd-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ceid=1295540)
---
Colorado's facing a lot of big problems. But when it comes to housing, we're in a full-blown crisis:
* The median sale cost of a home has nearly doubled over the last decade.
* Minimum-wage earners must work an 89-hour week to afford an apartment at fair market rent.
* Over half of renters here are cost-burdened.
* Eviction filings in 2024 skyrocketed more than 300% from 2020.

Falling supply and rising costs have put home ownership out of reach for too many and pushed Coloradans to live farther from their jobs. And the brunt of this crisis is shouldered by the most vulnerable in our communities.
So if we're going to make Colorado the best state to live, work and raise a family, **we have to ensure Coloradans can afford to live where they work without spending more than 30% of their income on housing.** And we all have a role to play in solving this crisis – including you, friend.
**_[So will you please take a moment to fill out my housing affordability survey b](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118972332/581499056/913257825?refcode=jtk963-surv-nd&code=jtk963-surv-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4OGI3ODkzOC04ZWJkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=n6TlizmP-4raa4HLxerDX3nyzfPb3sXl9dh8HJs9GwI=&emci=3be18d29-b2bc-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&emdi=88b78938-8ebd-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ceid=1295540)efore midnight tonight_**** _[? I can't fine-tune my ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118972335/581499064/913257825?refcode=jtk963-surv-nd&code=jtk963-surv-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4OGI3ODkzOC04ZWJkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=n6TlizmP-4raa4HLxerDX3nyzfPb3sXl9dh8HJs9GwI=&emci=3be18d29-b2bc-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&emdi=88b78938-8ebd-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ceid=1295540)plan to best meet your needs without first running it by you._**
Thank you in advance for chiming in here,

Michael

clean_body: I have a plan to tackle our housing affordability crisis | | | = If Colorado's going to provide the vision this country needs, friend, then as governor, it's on me to ensure we first meet Coloradans' most basic needs. I'm talking about the bottom tier of the hierarchy. The must-haves for survival. Food. Water. Air. Housing. But when rent eats up an entire paycheck (or more) every month, folks don’t have much left to meet other basic needs, let alone have economic security and safety. I have a plan to tackle our housing affordability crisis, but I need to make sure it meets the needs of the people I'm running to serve – people like you and 4,999 more members of this grassroots team. | | Colorado's facing a lot of big problems. But when it comes to housing, we're in a full-blown crisis: The median sale cost of a home has nearly doubled over the last decade. Minimum-wage earners must work an 89-hour week to afford an apartment at fair market rent. Over half of renters here are cost-burdened. Eviction filings in 2024 skyrocketed more than 300% from 2020. Falling supply and rising costs have put home ownership out of reach for too many and pushed Coloradans to live farther from their jobs. And the brunt of this crisis is shouldered by the most vulnerable in our communities. So if we're going to make Colorado the best state to live, work and raise a family, we have to ensure Coloradans can afford to live where they work without spending more than 30% of their income on housing. And we all have a role to play in solving this crisis – including you, friend. midnight tonight to best meet your needs without first running it by you. Thank you in advance for chiming in here, Michael | Bennet for Governor PO Box 270117 Louisville, CO 80027 United States Paid for by Bennet for Governor. Registered Agent: Tracie Moore. subject: Chris Pappas is a proven fighter, folks
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
Let me cut to the chase.
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97136?refcode=em20251109-9684&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9684%2E614464%2Et9i9Wm)
Peter — JB Pritzker here.
Let me cut to the chase: **New Hampshire’s Senate race could decide whether Democrats take back the majority — _and Republicans know it._**
Donald Trump and the MAGA GOP see this seat as one of their best shots to expand their power.
They’ve already got a Republican governor in New Hampshire, and they’re betting they can flip the Senate seat next. If they win here, our chances of taking back the Senate drop to zero.
_We can’t let that happen._
Chris Pappas is a proven fighter for working families — taking on Big Pharma, defending reproductive freedom, and making sure our government actually works for the people it serves.
I’ve seen what happens when leaders like Chris have the platform and the power to make change — and _I want him in the Senate._
I know Chris can win, but there’s no way around it: **_This isn’t a safe blue seat, folks._**
The GOP is ready to flood New Hampshire with attack ads and dark money. The only way to beat them is with early, grassroots support. That’s where you come in, Peter:
**[If you can, please split a $10 donation right now between Chris Pappas and Heartland Patriots to put Democrats on the path to a Senate majority.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/97137?refcode=em20251109-9684&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=10&t=2&akid=9684%2E614464%2Et9i9Wm)**

name: Trisha Calvarese
subject: Boebert’s death warrant for thousands of Coloradans
disclaimer_text: paid for by taxpayers, it's SICK
body:
Boebert is cavalier about all of this and in a Zoom conference this week she said her constituents' health is “not my job” and for people affected by the cuts to “get a peloton and a gym membership.”
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001_CAtUK9B4HMpEKRYEe9WLcSKr70Lc60AFI4ZNGq0-KUwsPYh6Ih9wVtpbi0dAmS3ONeMdoKzFkhZLutu_VPpRnH3yCuWwlhax08FSS3R6-7_Q4NGAJxyeuePq9zYbNMXQStVSkfahxuQ8osniosShy5yipZpmRKV80M4A5F2NMMuIQ6CSW0AfY8cuv5hfulzoeL_6SMd4_-5JJW_QSyKZYxHUrh93s81_ycKzaU93cg=&c=InhMRlrdeHTdi7BpcXVh9hGnPL0afSLmBvaNrf8CmTU4bhlGrvXfLw==&ch=f8xo6V99NpYvofIHCnldqZEpI7t4uZFUiNwaGFCH_DTCBuTiO-oNeA==)
---
|

Peter,
Earlier this summer, Lauren Boebert signed death warrants for tens of thousands of Coloradans when she voted to slash Medicaid and kick people off their private insurance with the Big Beautiful Bill.
It's the biggest federal rollback of healthcare in U.S. history, and it means cutting short tens of millions American lives.
Boebert is cavalier about it, in a Zoom conference she said her constituents' health is "not my job" and for people affected by the cuts to "get a peloton and a gym membership."
---
| [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001_CAtUK9B4HMpEKRYEe9WLcSKr70Lc60AFI4ZNGq0-KUwsPYh6Ih9wVtpbi0dAmS3ONeMdoKzFkhZLutu_VPpRnH3yCuWwlhax08FSS3R6-7_Q4NGAJxyeuePq9zYbNMXQStVSkfahxuQ8osniosShy5yipZpmRKV80M4A5F2NMMuIQ6CSW0AfY8cuv5hfulzoeL_6SMd4_-5JJW_QSyKZYxHUrh93s81XhdM9gUjKgY=&c=InhMRlrdeHTdi7BpcXVh9hGnPL0afSLmBvaNrf8CmTU4bhlGrvXfLw==&ch=f8xo6V99NpYvofIHCnldqZEpI7t4uZFUiNwaGFCH_DTCBuTiO-oNeA==)
---
Coming from someone who has the best health care in the country, paid for by taxpayers, it's SICK. I lost both parents to cancer days apart. It's offensive to families like mine that Boebert finds joy and makes jokes about the suffering she voted for.
Lauren Boebert is going to lose her job because I'm going to take it, the moment to make this happen is now. The GOP's favorability has dipped to record lows and even rural districts like mine are being seen as extremely flippable.
[Boebert is vulnerable and we need to press our advantage while we have it. Our team is laser focused on catching up and surpassing Boebert in fundraising and we need your help to get there. Can I count on you to rush a donation of $25 or even $5 to help us beat Boebert?](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001_CAtUK9B4HMpEKRYEe9WLcSKr70Lc60AFI4ZNGq0-KUwsPYh6Ih9wVtpbi0dAmS3VY9Ewf4Z_1IoDpTFQgfAdwno2m2QuUvvfSRl2wal_IyFlTW4Y3_W3q0KZSAQrki8l4gSiEXdrsmPqq-S5CJcuW86aW5xJigcCd6kkobCPCrQeUaSyZWA0i-I6ZZXNQ3__2ui54phGdM42cYZK5jE-gK9cu-hOz-x1SQcsQBnzL3B49KU8Pfkag==&c=InhMRlrdeHTdi7BpcXVh9hGnPL0afSLmBvaNrf8CmTU4bhlGrvXfLw==&ch=f8xo6V99NpYvofIHCnldqZEpI7t4uZFUiNwaGFCH_DTCBuTiO-oNeA==)

name: Team Warner
subject: You won’t believe this​​, Derek
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
body:
|
---

| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118973354/581503339/-165121341?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_Loomer_251109_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkZWYyODI1My05MGJkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Thq3_9x1A8zMdQCJiHkgwa4beXIrvjsIpM-loC6YJrs=) Derek, you won’t believe this. **The Pentagon credentialed right-wing conspiracy theorist and 9/11-denier Laura Loomer to cover President Trump’s Department of Defense.** This move by the Pentagon comes on the heels of Laura Loomer:
1. Throwing a tantrum so intense about Senator Warner’s planned, routine meeting with national intelligence professionals that the Trump administration CANCELED Mark’s visit

2. Calling for Senator Warner to be removed from office, tried for treason, and even labeling him a “liar” and “communist FRAUD”

3. Launching an online smear campaign against Mark, fueling far-right outrage online

What else will Laura Loomer throw our way now that she’s a credentialed member of the Pentagon Press Corps?

Senator Warner will always speak out about every dangerous, irresponsible move the Trump administration makes – no matter what online bullies like Laura Loomer have to say about it.

subject: We fight the fights others won’t
body:

like you — not CEOs, billionaires, or lobbyists. Our average donation is
$16, and every contribution helps us push for bold, progressive change.

communities. Will you pitch in today to power the Squad and grow our
movement? CHIP IN $16. Image of Alexandria, Ayanna, Ilhan, and Rashida.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe:

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.squadvictoryfund.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=1616%2E342411%2EjOR19A

Paid for by The Squad Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

In addition to what’s legally required, The Squad Victory Fund does not accept contributions from: (1) corporations, associations, or their PACs; (2) individuals registered as federal lobbyists or under FARA; (3) from the PACs, lobbyists, or SEC-named executives of fossil fuel companies; or (4) from the Fraternal Order of Police.

Contributions or gifts to The Squad Victory Fund are not tax deductible.

Contributions to The Squad Victory Fund will be allocated equally among: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

Any contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if any contributor makes a contribution that, when allocated, would exceed the amount that the contributor may lawfully give to any participant.

After contributing to The Squad Victory Fund, you will automatically be signed up to receive email updates from Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, and Rashida Tlaib for Congress. You may of course opt-out by unsubscribing at any time.

To contribute via check, please address to The Squad Victory Fund c/o MBA Consulting Group, PO Box 15845, Washington D.C., 20003

Email us: info@squadvictoryfund.com

name: The Resistance Lab via Team Jayapal
subject: Just one week left to RSVP for Resistance Lab!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
Sign up to attend or host a training next Sunday.
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20251109-5612-footer&t=1&akid=5612%2E420207%2EjkhhRe)
Hi Peter, Team Pramila here. We wanted to reach out to make sure you didn’t miss this!
**Our next Resistance Lab is one week from today** — and whether you’re attending solo, gathering a few friends to host a local session, or still on the fence about signing up, [**now’s the time to RSVP if you haven’t yet.**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/50885?utm_source=em20251109-5612&t=2&akid=5612%2E420207%2EjkhhRe)
This training is one of the most impactful ways you can be ready to resist authoritarianism and help build a strong, people-powered movement.
We truly hope to see you there — the stakes are too high to sit this one out!
— Team Jayapal
\-------- Forwarded message --------
From: The Resistance Lab via Team Jayapal
Subject: This is *not* your average Zoom meeting
Peter,
Authoritarianism is on the rise — and it’s never been more urgent for us to build power together.
That’s why Pramila launched the **Resistance Lab,** a grassroots training series to help people like you understand how democracies fall, how everyday people have fought back with nonviolent resistance, and what we can do _right now_ to resist authoritarianism in our own communities.
**Our next training is coming up on Sunday, November 16, and you can attend virtually OR host a local training session with your friends, family, or community.[Click here to RSVP here or sign up to host a training now!](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/50885?utm_source=em20251109-5612&t=3&akid=5612%2E420207%2EjkhhRe)**
[](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/50885?utm_source=em20251109-5612&t=4&akid=5612%2E420207%2EjkhhRe)
**[RSVP For The Resistance Lab](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/50885?utm_source=em20251109-5612&t=5&akid=5612%2E420207%2EjkhhRe)**
This is _not_ your average Zoom meeting. Resistance Lab was developed with global experts in democratic backsliding and non-violent resistance movements around the world, in combination with Pramila’s 20+ years of frontline experience organizing for justice.
**You’ll walk away with:**
* A deeper understanding of how authoritarianism rises
* Proven tactics used by nonviolent resistance movements to fight back
* Immediate, concrete actions you can take to organize in your community

And if you’re thinking about hosting a session but not sure you’re qualified, don’t worry! You don’t need to be an expert — just someone who’s passionate about standing up against authoritarianism.
When you sign up to host, we’ll send you everything you need: tech help, discussion guides, and a step-by-step agenda. Just pick a spot (your home, a café, a community center, etc.), and a way to stream the Zoom session (a TV and computer work great). We’ll handle the rest!
**More than 15,000 people in all 50 states have already joined the Resistance Lab.** Whether you’re joining solo or bringing your community, this training is for you. So please spread the word, invite your people, and help build this resistance movement together.
**[RSVP now to secure your spot for our Resistance Lab training on Sunday, November 16th.](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/50885?utm_source=em20251109-5612&t=6&akid=5612%2E420207%2EjkhhRe)**
In solidarity,
Team Jayapal
**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20251109-5612-footer&t=7&refcode2=5612_420207_jkhhRe&akid=5612%2E420207%2EjkhhRe)**
---
[](https://www.facebook.com/pramila.jayapal) | [](https://twitter.com/pramilajayapal) | [](https://www.instagram.com/pramilajayapal/)
Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, and small-dollar contributions sustain our movement for transformative change. To contribute by check, please address to Pramila for Congress, PO Box 21912, Seattle, WA 98111.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/did-your-info-change/?t=8&akid=5612%2E420207%2EjkhhRe)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=9&akid=5612%2E420207%2EjkhhRe)

Paid for by Pramila for Congress

name: Donald J. Trump
subject: I believe He saved me for a reason.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Never Surrender, Inc
body:
This is why I think He did it.
| | | | [](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
---
**I was never supposed to be President.**
Friend,
I was never supposed to survive the bullet of an assassin, **BUT I DID.**
I was never supposed to be President of the United States again, **BUT I AM.**
I was never supposed to Make America Great Again, **BUT I WILL NEVER SURRENDER!**
The great behemoth known as the Democrat Party threw everything at me: Hoaxes, Witch Hunts, Impeachments, Election Interference, Raids, Arrests, and so much more!
But the steady compass of Faith has guided my hands through it all.
NOW, the government is completely shut down, and they want **MAGA TO BE DONE.**
_We can’t take our foot off the gas!_
I was saved for this one reason: to Make America Great Again.
I was made for this, **BUT MAGA IS POWERED BY YOU.**
_That’s why I’ve launched a 24 HOUR TRUMP FUNDRAISING BLITZ, and I’m asking everyone to chip in $15 to make it one for the record books!_
Every time they count us out – count YOU out – we come back bigger, better, and stronger than ever before.
This is our moment to show the world that we will NEVER back down, NEVER give an inch, and NEVER SURRENDER in our fight to save this country.
**_[So if you’re with me all the way to the end, join with MILLIONS of your fellow PATRIOTS by chipping in $15 to keep the wins rolling in!>](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)_**

[GIVE $15](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
[STAND WITH TRUMP](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
May God Bless you,

Donald J. Trump

President of the United States

name: Powered by People
subject: An inside look at our work
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY POWERED BY PEOPLE
body:
[1]Powered By People

When we tell you your donation goes to helping more Texans vote, we mean
it. Just look at some of the interactions our volunteers had with voters
during last week’s election:

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251109.qYRymB&refcode=ema20251109-240452&t=1002&akid=240452%2E444211%2EmPg7hH ]Voter texting Powered by People volunteer with 'I voted' stickers in
Dallas County

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251109.qYRymB&refcode=ema20251109-240452&t=1003&akid=240452%2E444211%2EmPg7hH ]Voter texting Powered by People volunteer picture of 'I voted' sticker

These volunteers don’t just hand out information—they build real
relationships and make voting personal. They help Texans register to vote,
create a plan to cast a ballot, and follow up with them through Election
Day to ensure their voice is heard.

They answer questions, clear up confusion, and make the process
accessible. Because when voters are informed and supported, they show up
and make their voices heard. And we know that these votes can add up to
victories for candidates who will fight for working people.

But none of it happens without this team and our volunteers. These
organizing efforts to ensure Democrats can win critical races up and down
the ballot in 2026 aren’t cheap. And it’s your support that powers the
events, training, materials, and tools our volunteers need to reach Texans
across the state.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251109.qYRymB&refcode=ema20251109-240452&amount=3&t=1004&akid=240452%2E444211%2EmPg7hH ]Powered by People has been successful because of grassroots supporters
like you,
which is
why we’re asking: Can you chip in $3 today to help us
connect with even more voters in the coming elections?

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251109.qYRymB&refcode=ema20251109-240452&amount=3&t=1023&akid=240452%2E444211%2EmPg7hH ]DONATE $3

Thank you,

Powered by People

Just look at some of the interactions our volunteers had with voters during last week’s election: [ ]Voter texting Powered by People volunteer with 'I voted' stickers in Dallas County [ ]Voter texting Powered by People volunteer picture of 'I voted' sticker These volunteers don’t just hand out information—they build real relationships and make voting personal. They help Texans register to vote, create a plan to cast a ballot, and follow up with them through Election Day to ensure their voice is heard. They answer questions, clear up confusion, and make the process accessible. Because when voters are informed and supported, they show up and make their voices heard. And we know that these votes can add up to victories for candidates who will fight for working people. But none of it happens without this team and our volunteers. These organizing efforts to ensure Democrats can win critical races up and down the ballot in 2026 aren’t cheap. And it’s your support that powers the events, training, materials, and tools our volunteers need to reach Texans across the state. [ ]Powered by People has been successful because of grassroots supporters like you, which is why we’re asking: Can you chip in $3 today to help us connect with even more voters in the coming elections? [ ]DONATE $3 Thank you, Powered by People

Moments like these shape lives, Friend. At last year's conference, a cadet from Texas A&M called the experience of lunch with WWII heroes "_truly inspirational -- something I will never forget._" At nearly every meal, young cadets will sit side-by-side with World War II heroes, listening to stories of Normandy, Iwo Jima, and the Chosin Reservoir - _thanks to you._ At the evening reception, post-9/11 veterans will raise a glass in fellowship - _thanks to you, Friend._

clean_body: Last chance! $10 = A beer for a hero Your gift ensures every veteran has a place at the table at American Valor | | | Friend, This weekend, nearly one hundred American veterans — from World War II to the War on Terror — are gathering in Washington, D.C. at the 28th Annual Veterans Conference. They aren’t just talking to each other. They’re sitting across the table from hundreds of cadets and midshipmen, America’s next generation of military leaders. Friend, you can make sure that table is set. It's a simple but meaningful way to honor these heroes at this year's conference. Moments like these shape lives, Friend. At last year's conference, a cadet from Texas A&M called the experience of lunch with WWII heroes "truly inspirational -- something I will never forget." At nearly every meal, young cadets will sit side-by-side with World War II heroes, listening to stories of Normandy, Iwo Jima, and the Chosin Reservoir - thanks to you. At the evening reception, post-9/11 veterans will raise a glass in fellowship - thanks to you, Friend. These simple moments carry a profound message: America remembers, honors, and thanks her veterans - and inspires the next generation to follow in their footsteps. Friend, A small gift from you creates a big memory for both sides. When a cadet hears about D-Day directly from the man who landed there, history becomes real. When a Vietnam hero raises a glass at our reception this Friday, he knows he's not forgotten. These small acts of generosity turn into powerful memories...meals become conversations...beers become toasts. And veterans realize America still remembers. Thanks to you, Friend, our heroes will not only share their stories but will know their service is still remembered and celebrated -- more than 80 years after many of them first answered the call. Tim Holbert President American Veterans Center P.S. Friend, the simplest gift -- a meal, a beer -- can leave the deepest impression on America's veterans. The mission of the American Veterans Center and the World War II Veterans Committee is to preserve and promote the legacy and experiences of America's veterans and active duty service personnel from World War II through today. (Rita Corbin for the _Catholic Worker_)
News of Tuesday night's many victories was refreshing, and long overdue. Still, we have to remember how there is to go. The last few days have brought fresh news of terrible injustices being committed by the government in our name. These will have to be remedied if we are who we say we are. Especially in a country where the governing regime claims to reflect and promote Christian values, we can't look away from the truth about what they are doing.
This first piece from the New York Times is hard to read. It's the most in-depth account I've seen about what happened to the men who were sent to El Salvador's notorious CECOT prison back in the spring:
> _They said they were punished in a dark room called the island, where they were trampled, kicked and forced to kneel for hours._
> _One man said officers thrust his head into a tank of water to simulate drowning. Another said he was forced to perform oral sex on guards wearing hoods._
> _They said they were told by officials that they would die in the Salvadoran prison, that the world had forgotten them._
> _When they could no longer take it, they said, they cut themselves, writing protest messages on sheets in blood._
[Gift link here](https://substack.com/redirect/0e39b365-954b-46c8-a990-c93d6f56327b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Of course, all of this began with our government's choice to send these men there. It was cruel, unnecessary, and often unjustified by any real evidence of criminal history. They chose to do it in order to scare others, and flex their own power. Someday there will have to be accountability.
The next story hits closer to home. Much of America wants to forget about coal miners, and the sacrifices they made over years in order to keep our country going. Trump called more attention to them than almost any other politician in recent years, but the cruel irony is that he is destroying the rules meant to protect them. CBS News just published this great piece the effect of silica as the miners are forced to go deeper into the earth:
> _[M]uch of the easy-to-reach coal has already been extracted in West Virginia and neighboring Virginia and Kentucky, forcing miners to use massive equipment to eat through walls of quartz-filled sandstone to reach the remaining thin coal seams. This creates excess dust laced with shards of silica, which also causes lung cancer and kidney disease.**It 's 20 times more toxic than coal dust, the major culprit of the past that often sickened older workers.** The silica crystals embed in miners' lungs, causing chronic inflammation and eventually irreversible scarring that peppers X-rays with chalky spots. **It leaves proud, once-strong men skinny and weak. They choke on their food and gasp after just a few steps** , cradling shiny cylinders that provide a lifeline of oxygen through tubes snaking into their nostrils._
> _" If you've ever about drowned -- or anybody's about drowned -- they know what I'm talking about because I go through that every morning," said Mark F. Powell, a fourth-generation miner from southern West Virginia who's upset with Trump's policies. "By the end of the day, I'm so tired. Sometimes I don't even eat supper. I'll come home and sometimes I'm not even able to take a shower. I'm not ashamed to tell it. I'll lay on the floor and go to sleep."_
A simple rule could stop this from happening to the next generation of miners, who may continue to work even if coal declines as a share of our power supply, since rare-earth elements and other needed resources could be extracted from coal seams long into the future. Read more about the silica rule, and Trump's efforts to get rid of it in order to make more money for the industry, [here](https://substack.com/redirect/144c691b-d70f-4c5a-8dab-b3c61f3ba82f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
And let all of this serve as motivation for the work it will take to build on Tuesday's success at the polls. Have a restful Sunday and a good week.

clean_body: It's important not to look away. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more It's important not to look away. | | | Nov 9 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- (Rita Corbin for the Catholic Worker) News of Tuesday night's many victories was refreshing, and long overdue. Still, we have to remember how there is to go. The last few days have brought fresh news of terrible injustices being committed by the government in our name. These will have to be remedied if we are who we say we are. Especially in a country where the governing regime claims to reflect and promote Christian values, we can't look away from the truth about what they are doing. This first piece from the New York Times is hard to read. It's the most in-depth account I've seen about what happened to the men who were sent to El Salvador's notorious CECOT prison back in the spring: They said they were punished in a dark room called the island, where they were trampled, kicked and forced to kneel for hours. One man said officers thrust his head into a tank of water to simulate drowning. Another said he was forced to perform oral sex on guards wearing hoods. They said they were told by officials that they would die in the Salvadoran prison, that the world had forgotten them. When they could no longer take it, they said, they cut themselves, writing protest messages on sheets in blood. Of course, all of this began with our government's choice to send these men there. It was cruel, unnecessary, and often unjustified by any real evidence of criminal history. They chose to do it in order to scare others, and flex their own power. Someday there will have to be accountability. The next story hits closer to home. Much of America wants to forget about coal miners, and the sacrifices they made over years in order to keep our country going. Trump called more attention to them than almost any other politician in recent years, but the cruel irony is that he is destroying the rules meant to protect them. CBS News just published this great piece the effect of silica as the miners are forced to go deeper into the earth: [M]uch of the easy-to-reach coal has already been extracted in West Virginia and neighboring Virginia and Kentucky, forcing miners to use massive equipment to eat through walls of quartz-filled sandstone to reach the remaining thin coal seams. This creates excess dust laced with shards of silica, which also causes lung cancer and kidney disease.It 's 20 times more toxic than coal dust, the major culprit of the past that often sickened older workers. The silica crystals embed in miners' lungs, causing chronic inflammation and eventually irreversible scarring that peppers X-rays with chalky spots. It leaves proud, once-strong men skinny and weak. They choke on their food and gasp after just a few steps , cradling shiny cylinders that provide a lifeline of oxygen through tubes snaking into their nostrils. " If you've ever about drowned -- or anybody's about drowned -- they know what I'm talking about because I go through that every morning," said Mark F. Powell, a fourth-generation miner from southern West Virginia who's upset with Trump's policies. "By the end of the day, I'm so tired. Sometimes I don't even eat supper. I'll come home and sometimes I'm not even able to take a shower. I'm not ashamed to tell it. I'll lay on the floor and go to sleep." A simple rule could stop this from happening to the next generation of miners, who may continue to work even if coal declines as a share of our power supply, since rare-earth elements and other needed resources could be extracted from coal seams long into the future. Read more about the silica rule, and Trump's efforts to get rid of it in order to make more money for the industry, . And let all of this serve as motivation for the work it will take to build on Tuesday's success at the polls. Have a restful Sunday and a good week. Conor Lamb is free today. But if you enjoyed this post, you can tell Conor Lamb that their writing is valuable by pledging a future subscription. You won't be charged unless they enable payments. | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Conor Lamb 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?

subject: I want to hear directly from you on this critical issue

body:
#
Peter,
Last month, we kicked off a series of public town halls to address the gun violence epidemic in Minnesota.
[](https://act.timwalz.com/survey/em_tw_251109/?t=1&akid=59%2E34582%2EcqwTmW)
I take this issue seriously — for too long, gun violence has gone unchecked, and it’s about time we do more to prevent it and save lives. Unfortunately, some in the Republican Party are still standing in the way.
That’s why I decided to bring this topic to the people and host these important conversations in districts where state legislators are on the fence about supporting our proposed assault-style weapons and high-capacity magazines ban.
**While we continue to hold more of these gun violence town halls in communities across the state, in case you can’t make it to one, I want to hear directly from you.**
**[Will you take our survey today to share your stories and priorities related to our fight to end gun violence in Minnesota?](https://act.timwalz.com/survey/em_tw_251109/?t=2&akid=59%2E34582%2EcqwTmW)**

With gratitude,
Tim

Under Governor Walz’s leadership, Minnesota is showing the world what’s possible when we come together with grit, compassion, and a shared sense of purpose.
We secured free school meals for every student, cut child poverty, passed Paid Family Leave, delivered record investments in education, and more.

name: David Hogg
subject: Akbar is headed to a runoff — help him cross the finish line
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
Your support can help push Akbar over the finish line on December 2nd.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20251109-1&t=1&akid=2822%2E587540%2ED12raz)
Hi Peter. David here with an update on one of the most important races for our movement:
**Akbar Ali is on the cusp of becoming the youngest legislator in Georgia history** — not by luck, but because he showed up. He knocked on doors, listened, and fought for his community. And he got here because supporters like you believed in him and fueled his campaign.
But Leaders We Deserve isn’t here for _“almost.”_ **We’re here to make history.**
No candidate reached 50% in the race for Georgia’s 106th House District, so _**Akbar is headed to a December 2nd runoff.**_ We only have a few weeks to finish this fight, and we’ve come way too far to fall short now.
**[Will you split a donation right now of $21 — for a 21-year-old candidate — or anything else between Leaders We Deserve and Akbar’s campaign to power this final push and win?](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251109_1_atlanta.ppmmcC&refcode=em20251109-2822&amount=21&t=3&akid=2822%2E587540%2ED12raz)**
**[Donate $21 »](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251109_1_atlanta.ppmmcC&amount=21&refcode=em20251109-2822&t=4&akid=2822%2E587540%2ED12raz)**
Akbar’s momentum is real. He finished _just 500 votes_ behind his runoff opponent — an extraordinary result for a first-time, 21-year-old candidate.
He earned it the hard way: months of door-knocking, building support conversation by conversation, making sure every community in one of the most diverse districts in the country was heard — even traveling with two interpreters to ensure no one was left out.
From those conversations, he built a platform rooted in what people here actually need: expanding Medicaid, protecting SNAP, improving public schools, and addressing everyday issues like predatory HOA fines — things you only understand when you’re truly rooted in the community you’re asking to represent.
And here’s the truth: **Akbar made it to this runoff because of you.** Your support turned a long shot into a frontrunner. Because of you, we were able to invest in field organizing and knock doors in Atlanta just days before Election Day. I talked to voters who _didn’t even know an election was happening_ — but after we talked, they were committed to voting for Akbar.
That’s how we win. One conversation at a time, one race at a time, building a new generation of leadership from the ground up.
Now we need to finish what we started. Runoffs are decided by turnout, and turnout is about organizing. Your support right now will determine what happens on December 2nd.
**[If you’re ready to help us make history, can you please split a contribution of $21 or anything you can give between Leaders We Deserve and Akbar’s campaign so we don’t fall short?](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251109_1_atlanta.ppmmcC&refcode=em20251109-2822&amount=21&t=5&akid=2822%2E587540%2ED12raz)**
**[Donate $21 »](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251109_1_atlanta.ppmmcC&amount=21&refcode=em20251109-2822&t=24&akid=2822%2E587540%2ED12raz)**
Thank you for being a part of this movement.
— David Hogg
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20251109-1&t=25&refcode2=2822_587540_D12raz&akid=2822%2E587540%2ED12raz)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=26&akid=2822%2E587540%2ED12raz)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=27&akid=2822%2E587540%2ED12raz)

To give by check:
Leaders We Deserve
611 Pennsylvania Ave SE
#192
Washington, DC 20003
Email us: [info@leaderswedeserve.com](mailto:info@leaderswedeserve.com)
PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE.COM, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE

White women voted for Abigail Spanberger in Virginia in overwhelming numbers because they wanted someone who looks like them.
Yes, Spanberger the democrat candidate and winner of Virginia's gubernatorial race is white, whereas Winsome Earle-Sears the republican candidate is black, but by "looks like them" I do not at all mean race.
I mean misery.
Across every demographic marker, the exit poll data from the recent election is clear: rich, white, highly educated, areligious, unmarried women vote more than 80% liberal. This archetype is also, just according to the data, something else: more depressed, over-medicated, with an increasing rate of suicide and the highest rates of drug and alcohol abuse than at any time in history.
Herstory, if you prefer.
* * *
* * *

The phenomenon of these deeply unhappy individuals is so commonly experienced in society that we have even given it a name: **" The Karen."**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/1bca3781-55ed-428c-8663-e4e585c9a642?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
And these Karens saw themselves in Spanberger who treated them as want to be treated: victims.
Virginia's Karens are victims of Donald Trump who twice has denied them an equally miserable woman Presidency. They are victims of Elon Musk's DOGE cuts to their government funded nonprofit which raises awareness about heart disease in lesbian snails. Victims of the "Republican shut down" which furloughed them from their Department of Whatever day job under the cruel moniker "non-essential personnel". Victims of male Justices on the Supreme Court who threatened their sacred right to abortion. Victims of War Secretary Pete Hegseth who said they must train at the same physical standards as men. (Pete perhaps they hate the most because no man who looks like Pete winks at them anymore).
Winsome Sears did not coddle these women. The former Marine did not indulge their Karenocity, feed their Karenness, ignite their Karenisms.
Sears, the black immigrant Republican, does not see herself as a victim. Spanberger did, as all liberal women do, and they loved her for it.
For a Karen, elections are not about who is the better leader or which policies best serve society. No, elections are a rage-filled debate on The View, a self- empowering episode of Oprah, or a candle-lit bathtub and a well-worn copy of Fifty Shades of Grey.
Electoral politics are affirmation. They are therapy.
Think about it: The pointless "No Kings" protest last month was mostly comprised of white women in their 40's, and that is very odd. This is not 1970's, where women are called "toots" with their fanny's smacked in smoke filled offices. These Karens are not Norma Rae demanding the right to unionize. Women have overcome legitimate trials, and triumphantly so. But that is not today's Karen who almost certainly has experienced nothing comparable without an immediate HR action, lawsuit, or expose in a print magazine.
Today's 40-year old women have only known a red-carpet life in the majority with every effort of institutions and corporations catering to their needs. The majority of college and university students are women. The majority of post- graduate and doctoral students, medical school, law school students are women. Women out-earn men in the same professional field, a statistic that makes them apparently so unhappy, they have to invent the "pay gap" myth.
No Kings? If anything, the protest should be called "No Queens". Women in America have it so good they can yell at a man for holding open a door but still demand they exit first in a fire.
Women have the privilege of publicly fighting the demons of their own making. Toxic masculinity. The patriarchy. Doña Quijote fighting the windmills of her mind.
Yet, despite all the progress women have made (now bordering on societal overcompensation) these women are still deeply unhappy, and that is a national concern. Fixing it is imperative, because these Karens are voting us into poverty, debt, war, climate change stupidity, open borders, rampant crime, or, tragically, even getting us killed.
No man was shocked to discover that the pilot of the Blackhawk helicopter which flew directly into the path of a passenger jet at Reagan Airport killing 67 people was a known political Karen who refused to listen to the male air traffic controllers.
Slay, queen.
Show me a terrible policy and I will show you the 40-something year old liberal woman who champions it.
It is Karens who push the trans agenda on children. Karens want open borders. Karens advocate for ending cash bail.
Regime change in Libya? Sure, it was four American men who were brutally murdered (and tens of thousands of Africans who are now sold for as little as $400 in the largest slave operation in human history including the transatlantic slave trade) but super-Karen Samantha Power thought it was a good idea.
Yes, white liberal women in America are going through a thing, and we are all suffering as a result.
Here in Virginia where I run my sheep farm I am all but guaranteed tax hikes. The Karens have willed it, and the incoming Spanberger Administration will destroy our strong state budget because, as a Karen herself, undisciplined spending will be her second nature. Just look at another Karen governor, Tim Walz of Minnesota, who turned an $18billion surplus into a $5billion deficit.
But look at me mansplaining... as if any of this even matters. Spanberger is historic! And the demographic that elected her can celebrate their collective victory. They will celebrate by staying miserable.
Voting liberal is does not make them happy. Insane liberal policies do not bring them joy. Karens voted for Spanberger to make us miserable. THAT is their joy. "If I am going to be miserable then you will be, too." Spanberger's election is not one that cures or calms or assuages pain. It is affirmation by dissemination, for when we are all as angry as they are, in our violent, expensive, lawless, overtaxed communities, then they aren't quite as singular in their misery as before.
All the aforementioned negative realities facing women are trending in the wrong way: the depression, drugs, medication. More liberalism, more government, more protests, more anger at Trump or the patriarchy or the unquestionably handsome Pete Hegseth... none of it will change that.
My concern is that it will continue to worsen. This unbreakable, engaged monolith voting bloc is very politically valuable. It is the new black vote in South Carolina, but it is found everywhere, and the candidates are much more at home drinking chardonnay than clapping at Church. Candidates will be forged like Uruk-hai in the pits of Isengard for the sole purpose of capturing the Karen vote, creating death spirals of government funding for awareness- raising and programs for marginalized communities... government ventures creating more Karens growing the bloc bigger and bigger...
Spanberger cannot make the Karens happy. She will just make the rest of us miserable. That is the goal
The government. Academia. Liberal philanthropy. All institutions which produce the Karen, all institutions which, in my next op-ed I will outline why they must be destroyed if we, as a free people, are to survive. As I encourage my London friends to get a dog and bacon delivery service, I encourage my friends here, men and women, black and white, young and old, protect yourself from the Karen because she is coming for you.
She is coming for us all because we have what she hates most of all: happiness.
Honor and courage.
* * *
_**Daniel Turner is the founder and executive director of Power The Future, a national nonprofit organization that advocates for American energy jobs. He also runs a sheep and cattle farm in rural Virginia. Contact him at daniel@powerthefuture.com and follow him on Twitter @DanielTurnerPTF**_

**The people of Arizona’s 3rd have not had representation for nearly 50 days because Speaker Johnson would rather protect the identity of abusers than bring accountability.** He has the power to put a stop to all of this — instead, he’s dragging his feet and leaving Arizonans unrepresented. It’s hypocritical, it’s undemocratic, and it’s bullshit.

name: Team Heinrich
subject: This could complicate your holiday travel plans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Martin Heinrich for Senate
body:
✈️ [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118975287/581510794/-272948131?refcode=AU_MTH_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20251109_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YmZiZjA0Yi05N2JkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=eBxZaylftppIlvVq1UzyvDxHnpxmRzJupDxoVOqr2mE=&emci=504b7112-6ebb-f011-8e61-6045bdd6f338&emdi=6bfbf04b-97bd-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ceid=252045)
---
Team — Holiday travel is set to break records in the U.S. this year, with major airlines reporting a _2.2% increase_ in Thanksgiving bookings vs. last year. ✈️ Yet bipartisan regulations that ensured airlines provide refunds for ticket fees, cancellations, and delays are being **rolled back** by the Department of Transportation (DOT) under Trump-appointed Secretary Sean Duffy. That’s why Martin Heinrich joined a coalition of 17 Senate Democrats to demand that Secretary Duffy stop targeting regulations that make flying**safer, affordable, and transparent** for passengers in New Mexico and nationwide. I'll be blunt, Peter: Sellout Stefanik is a self-serving politician who cares more about Donald Trump's approval than what's best for New York families.

She supports Trump's expensive tariffs that are jacking up the costs of everything from clothing to groceries for New Yorkers. She voted multiple times against the child tax credit.

Stefanik brags about being the "deciding vote" for his agenda that will rip away health care from 1.5 million New Yorkers and put five rural hospitals in her district on the chopping block.

And when the Trump administration threatened to cut New York police funding, Stefanik stayed silent.

**New Yorkers need a governor who will stand up to Trump and fight like hell for them.**

clean_body: I have some news to share. | = Peter, I have some news to share: Republican Elise Stefanik just announced her campaign for governor. And Stefanik's special interest donors have already rushed her $12 million to take me down and flip New York red. I've never backed down from a fight — and I sure as hell won't start now. Stefanik will be watching us closely, and we need to usher in a record-breaking fundraising push to show she doesn't stand a chance. = | = I'll be blunt, Peter: Sellout Stefanik is a self-serving politician who cares more about Donald Trump's approval than what's best for New York families. She supports Trump's expensive tariffs that are jacking up the costs of everything from clothing to groceries for New Yorkers. She voted multiple times against the child tax credit. Stefanik brags about being the "deciding vote" for his agenda that will rip away health care from 1.5 million New Yorkers and put five rural hospitals in her district on the chopping block. And when the Trump administration threatened to cut New York police funding, Stefanik stayed silent. New Yorkers need a governor who will stand up to Trump and fight like hell for them. **=** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Thank you and ever upward, Kathy = | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List.
