---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/mansharamani/p/rambling-and-remembering?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/da42eadf-a92a-4769-8c8f-2dc6fe8a350b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
This week was another spent on the road, and another spent dealing with cancellations and delays. So, as I cooled my jets in airport lounges waiting for the airlines to get their act together, I got caught up on recent political headlines.
The off-year elections were held on Tuesday, and it surely seems like it was a very good day for the Democrats. While the media has been trumpeting this as a harbinger for the looming mid-terms, I don't know how much we should really read into Democrat wins in..., well, Democrat areas. New York City? New Jersey? Hardly Red bastions. But the margins of victory are still noteworthy. The Democrat gubernatorial candidate received nearly 60 percent of the vote in [New Jersey](https://substack.com/redirect/dd1b7a56-29c5-4db9-b714-754505bc45be?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as a super-charged Democrat base came out to vote in droves.
Looking forward, as a general rule, the party that holds the White House generally does poorly in the mid-terms. This was true in Virginia and it appears many federal employees who reside in northern Virginia and remain out of work due to the historically-long government shutdown voiced their dissatisfaction at the polls...and took it out on Republicans.
One the most surprising outcomes of last Tuesday night was the election of Jay Jones as Virginia's Attorney General, defeating a GOP incumbent. Jones had been embroiled in a scandal and as some of his [violent texts](https://substack.com/redirect/9c63d5d0-c6e1-4186-99c7-1c39587c949d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) came to light, the outcome of the race seemed to be less and less certain. Jones ended up winning nearly 53 percent of the vote. I can't help but be a little saddened by the result...if [fantasizing about violence](https://substack.com/redirect/0f564601-32c5-4fa3-9e3c-d20a207a2ed5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) against your political opponents and their families isn't disqualifying, then what is? It unfortunately reveals the coarseness (and hypocrisy) of today's politics as many voters readily excuse deficiencies of their own political tribe in the interest of beating the other.
In New York City, the election victory of avowed socialist [Zohran Mamdani](https://substack.com/redirect/cb7dc4ef-ac70-4e65-9170-5481c51aa4a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is sending shockwaves throughout the country. Mamdani is emblematic of the young, far-left progressive wing of the Democrat party that appears to be ascendent. During the campaign he championed ideas such as a rent freeze, fare-free city buses, and free city-run grocery stores, ideas that appear archaic to those of us who lived through the Cold War and yet fresh and innovative to those who did not. (I find it ironic that the proposition of a government run grocery store was compelling to voters...during a government shut down!) Mamdani's election inspired a bunch of admittedly politicized yet hilarious videos, including the following one which caught my attention.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/ccd09df9-c0ed-492a-b49f-6111d419630e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Regardless, his election is surely a sign of the times, where dissatisfaction with affordability, among other galvanizing topics, is driving action. Might this be pointing to an [AOC run](https://substack.com/redirect/b7020e11-42f7-4a33-a933-0e25de2a88c1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for U.S. Senate? Or even president? Regardless, these dynamics squarely point to a very divisive presidential primary process for the Democrats -- generational and idealogical battle lines have been drawn -- and a headwind for the party's prospects to reclaim the White House in 2028.
_**Enough rambling, time for remembering.**_
Former Vice President Dick Cheney [passed away this week](https://substack.com/redirect/6ab156bd-a605-485f-8c91-9e72793810f9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). His public service career spanned decades and had several iterations...as a member of congress, presidential chief of staff, the U.S. Secretary of Defense who guided the country through the First Gulf War, and then finally as the Vice President who was the architect of the American response to 9/11. I always admired his intellect and his calm dispassionate approach to debate and persuasion; his [takedown](https://substack.com/redirect/65568513-6766-47df-ab71-989cf66677c9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of his opponent in the 2004 vice presidential debate is the stuff of legend.
As Sarah Lawrence College Professor of Politics Samuel Abrams highlighted, Cheney was about far more than mere service: "...[beyond the offices he held, Cheney represented something far rarer in modern public life; a disciplined servant of the republic who carried the burdens of leadership without seeking applause](https://substack.com/redirect/9db767b3-75f3-4731-b4d2-14995edc5649?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)."
As an intern at the [American Enterprise Institute](https://substack.com/redirect/53753b05-71eb-445f-ac14-2d060b41b31d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the early 1990s, I had a chance to get to know Dick and his wife Lynne, and fondly recall many conversations about the destructive dynamics around "political correctness," the predecessor to the woke fever that seems finally to be breaking. They were both always gracious, kind, supportive, and encouraging of me. My last substantive conversation with him was in 2019 when we spoke at length about the threat of an increasingly aggressive China at an event in the Middle East at which we were keynote speakers.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/808abfd3-2497-4e8b-b1d7-9bf776a209bb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
While this is clearly a controversial view today, I agree with AEI senior fellow Michael Rubin who wrote in the _National Security Journal_ that "[History Will Vindicate Dick Cheney](https://substack.com/redirect/fbca6a59-55ce-4f5f-b3d5-b227d72999ad?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)." Cheney was the consummate public servant, led with values, and didn't change his stance based on the latest polls. As Rubin summarizes: "Ch[eney often said he made the best decisions with the intelligence available at the time...While the CIA likes to depict its intelligence on Iraq as clear and suggest Cheney fabricated a pretext for war, the reality was that CIA reports often pointed in multiple directions and were full of if's and but's...](https://substack.com/redirect/fbca6a59-55ce-4f5f-b3d5-b227d72999ad?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)"
As I've often noted in my talks about navigating uncertainty, the essence of leadership is making a decision with the best available information at the time. The future is never certain and outcomes often differ (sometimes materially) from predictions. Leaders need to gather diverse perspectives, think in terms of multiple futures, triangulate different proposed actions, and ultimately... lead. Cheney did that.
America needs more independent thinkers who are dedicated to the nation. [As Yuval Levin wrote in the ](https://substack.com/redirect/23a61335-9754-4544-add2-9115fd89d1ab?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_**[National Review](https://substack.com/redirect/23a61335-9754-4544-add2-9115fd89d1ab?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_[,](https://substack.com/redirect/23a61335-9754-4544-add2-9115fd89d1ab?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
> [A free society always depends on the willingness of some of its ablest people to spend their lives in the service of the nation as public officials. People who understand themselves as providing that kind of service to their country stand out in our democracy. They are surely moved by the usual mix of motives that drive people into political life: ambition, confidence, curiosity, a desire to make a difference and to be known for making a difference, love of country. But they stand out because they tend to serve in a peculiar variety of offices over an unusually long stretch of time and to embody each of them by taking its distinct purpose and form seriously. America has always been fortunate to have such people in its public life. But their number has diminished over time, so those we have gotten to see in our own lifetimes really stand out.](https://substack.com/redirect/23a61335-9754-4544-add2-9115fd89d1ab?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Dick Cheney was a dedicated public servant, "[a gentleman of power and principle](https://substack.com/redirect/9db767b3-75f3-4731-b4d2-14995edc5649?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)," and a man who blended conviction with civility. He understood politics was about responsibility (not resentment) and leadership was about values (not polls). And perhaps most importantly, he unapologetically put America first.

---|---|---
**VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI** is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. **He 's currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza**. _LinkedIn_ has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and _Worth_ profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, _**[The Making of a Generalist: An Independent Thinker Finds Unconventional Success in an Uncertain World](https://substack.com/redirect/67265867-ba94-4953-a7bc-ec5972bab14f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ , _**[Think for Yourself: Restoring Common Sense in an Age of Experts and Artificial Intelligence](https://substack.com/redirect/a9c8def6-0131-48ae-a647-d484ac83aa9b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ and _**[Boombustology: Spotting Financial Bubbles Before They Burst](https://substack.com/redirect/101b40f1-32f1-4afe-a5a5-586e0c449acb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_. Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing.
You're currently a free subscriber to [Navigating Uncertainty](https://substack.com/redirect/ae862e03-6722-4af3-92cf-4056db6c4e4a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). clean_body: Reflecting on the week's developments and remembering an American leader and patriot. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? Reflecting on the week's developments and remembering an American leader and patriot. | | | Nov 9 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- This week was another spent on the road, and another spent dealing with cancellations and delays. So, as I cooled my jets in airport lounges waiting for the airlines to get their act together, I got caught up on recent political headlines. The off-year elections were held on Tuesday, and it surely seems like it was a very good day for the Democrats. While the media has been trumpeting this as a harbinger for the looming mid-terms, I don't know how much we should really read into Democrat wins in..., well, Democrat areas. New York City? New Jersey? Hardly Red bastions. But the margins of victory are still noteworthy. The Democrat gubernatorial candidate received nearly 60 percent of the vote in as a super-charged Democrat base came out to vote in droves. Looking forward, as a general rule, the party that holds the White House generally does poorly in the mid-terms. This was true in Virginia and it appears many federal employees who reside in northern Virginia and remain out of work due to the historically-long government shutdown voiced their dissatisfaction at the polls...and took it out on Republicans. One the most surprising outcomes of last Tuesday night was the election of Jay Jones as Virginia's Attorney General, defeating a GOP incumbent. Jones had been embroiled in a scandal and as some of his came to light, the outcome of the race seemed to be less and less certain. Jones ended up winning nearly 53 percent of the vote. I can't help but be a little saddened by the result...if against your political opponents and their families isn't disqualifying, then what is? It unfortunately reveals the coarseness (and hypocrisy) of today's politics as many voters readily excuse deficiencies of their own political tribe in the interest of beating the other. In New York City, the election victory of avowed socialist is sending shockwaves throughout the country. Mamdani is emblematic of the young, far-left progressive wing of the Democrat party that appears to be ascendent. During the campaign he championed ideas such as a rent freeze, fare-free city buses, and free city-run grocery stores, ideas that appear archaic to those of us who lived through the Cold War and yet fresh and innovative to those who did not. (I find it ironic that the proposition of a government run grocery store was compelling to voters...during a government shut down!) Mamdani's election inspired a bunch of admittedly politicized yet hilarious videos, including the following one which caught my attention. Regardless, his election is surely a sign of the times, where dissatisfaction with affordability, among other galvanizing topics, is driving action. Might this be pointing to an for U.S. Senate? Or even president? Regardless, these dynamics squarely point to a very divisive presidential primary process for the Democrats -- generational and idealogical battle lines have been drawn -- and a headwind for the party's prospects to reclaim the White House in 2028. Enough rambling, time for remembering. Former Vice President Dick Cheney . His public service career spanned decades and had several iterations...as a member of congress, presidential chief of staff, the U.S. Secretary of Defense who guided the country through the First Gulf War, and then finally as the Vice President who was the architect of the American response to 9/11. I always admired his intellect and his calm dispassionate approach to debate and persuasion; his of his opponent in the 2004 vice presidential debate is the stuff of legend. As Sarah Lawrence College Professor of Politics Samuel Abrams highlighted, Cheney was about far more than mere service: "...." As an intern at the in the early 1990s, I had a chance to get to know Dick and his wife Lynne, and fondly recall many conversations about the destructive dynamics around "political correctness," the predecessor to the woke fever that seems finally to be breaking. They were both always gracious, kind, supportive, and encouraging of me. My last substantive conversation with him was in 2019 when we spoke at length about the threat of an increasingly aggressive China at an event in the Middle East at which we were keynote speakers. | | ---|---|--- While this is clearly a controversial view today, I agree with AEI senior fellow Michael Rubin who wrote in the National Security Journal that "." Cheney was the consummate public servant, led with values, and didn't change his stance based on the latest polls. As Rubin summarizes: "Ch" As I've often noted in my talks about navigating uncertainty, the essence of leadership is making a decision with the best available information at the time. The future is never certain and outcomes often differ (sometimes materially) from predictions. Leaders need to gather diverse perspectives, think in terms of multiple futures, triangulate different proposed actions, and ultimately... lead. Cheney did that. America needs more independent thinkers who are dedicated to the nation. Dick Cheney was a dedicated public servant, "," and a man who blended conviction with civility. He understood politics was about responsibility (not resentment) and leadership was about values (not polls). And perhaps most importantly, he unapologetically put America first. | | ---|---|--- VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. He 's currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza. LinkedIn has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and Worth profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, , and . Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing. You're currently a free subscriber to . For the full experience, | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Kelan Publishing, LLC PO BOX 932, Lincoln, NH 03251 ? ?

The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Legal Fund. A contributor may designate his or her contribution for a particular participant. Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party. Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy. = = = mailto:info@roycooper.com | Cooper Victory Fund PO Box 1190 Raleigh, NC 27602 Paid for by Cooper Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Cooper for North Carolina, DSCC, and North Carolina Democratic Party — Federal. Contributions or gifts to Cooper Victory Fund are not tax deductible. The first $7,000 of each contribution from a person and the first $10,000 from a multicandidate committee (“PAC”) will be allocated to Cooper for North Carolina. The first $3,500 of a person’s contribution and the first $5,000 of a PAC’s contribution will be designated for the primary election; the next $3,500/$5,000 will be designated for the general election. The next $10,000 from a person and $5,000 from a PAC will be allocated to North Carolina Democratic Party – Federal. The next $44,300 of a contribution from a person and the next $15,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC General Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Headquarters Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Legal Fund. A contributor may designate his or her contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if following it would result in an excessive contribution. Any donor history information in this email reflects what we have on file for this specific email address. If you have donated with a different email address, with a check, or with a spouse — thank you so much! We have that on file and cannot thank you enough for supporting this campaign. By clicking the links above, you certify that you are responsible for paying all charges incurred in using the debit or credit card to be charged, and that your personal funds will be the true source of the contribution.

This Wednesday is the deadline -- and our baby girl is leading the charge [to make a difference.](https://substack.com/redirect/dfd64f81-9e30-4329-b852-0252264a3769?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
She is an elite athlete -- training six days a week. She takes honors and AP classes and carries a perfect 4.0 GPA. She's disciplined, kind, and focused -- the kind of young woman who believes hard work and heart can change the world.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/dfd64f81-9e30-4329-b852-0252264a3769?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Now she's putting that same drive into something bigger than herself. She's serving as a team leader for her school's **Mini-THON** , a student-led movement that raises funds and awareness for **[Four Diamonds](https://substack.com/redirect/dfd64f81-9e30-4329-b852-0252264a3769?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[,](https://substack.com/redirect/dfd64f81-9e30-4329-b852-0252264a3769?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) an organization devoted to conquering childhood cancer.
These kids train, organize, and fundraise -- not for themselves, but for children fighting the hardest battle of all.
Will you help her reach her goal before the Wednesday deadline?
We couldn't be prouder of her -- not just for her achievements, but for her heart. Thank you for standing with her, and with every family praying for a cure.
**For the kids. For the future. For the fight.**

name: Beth Farnham for Congress
subject: District Round-up 11.9.25
body:

# [District Round‐up 11.9.25](https://public-api.wordpress.com/bar/?stat=groovemails-events&bin=wpcom_email_click&redirect_to=http%3A%2F%2Fbethfarnhamforcongress.com%2F2025%2F11%2F09%2Fdistrict-round-up-11-9-25%2F%3Fjetpack_skip_subscription_popup&sr=0&signature=802e13a9909bc0de825c515e62dc8c83&blog_id=213069660&user=248418217&_&_z=z)
| By **Elizabeth Farnham** on November 9, 2025
---
Dear Neighbor,
Thank you for reading my District Round-up. As your candidate for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District, it is my honor and pleasure to deliver to you where I’ve been and what issues I’ve tackled in the past week. Criss-crossing the counties of Adams, Franklin, Fulton, Bedford, Cambria, Blair, Huntingdon, Mifflin, Juniata, Perry, Cumberland, and Somerset gives me great opportunities to meet with voters, hear what is on your hearts and minds, and the chance to voice my fight for Everyday Americans. If you would like to contribute to my campaign, please donate here: [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beth-fa,rnham-for-congress-1](https://bethfarnhamforcongress.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=a5a5d472c3356c6c1f2149b8258361e1817e2261c239acf8d1427288b7207956&blog_id=213069660&post_id=5491&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=56e5bfcc94be1b6acd117e36d2162c64&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYWN0Ymx1ZS5jb20vZG9uYXRlL2JldGgtZmFybmhhbS1mb3ItY29uZ3Jlc3MtMQ=&email_id=2c242be240de29cb973db19e3e4c3afc)
On Tuesday, voters across the country solidly rebuked the disasterous Trump administration. Americans don't want what the Republicans offer - LOSS. Loss of food assistance, healthcare, jobs, national security, due process, human rights, and so much more is hurting Everyday Americans. Everyday Americans want leaders in their corner, not billionaires who sit around giving each other tax cuts.
All Trump and his cronies gave us that day was another day of Republican shutdown.
But...
In Pennsylvania, Justice prevailed.
Congratulations to Justices Donahue, Dougherty, Wecht, Beck Dubow, Wojick, Neuman, and Tsai! Pennsylvanians will continue to have reproductive rights and fair districts!
In Gettysburg, Johnstown, Chambersburg, Shippensburg, Hollidaysburg, Straban Township, Cumberland County, and Greencastle, Democrats won competitive races.
Congratulations to Chad-Alan Carr, Sylvia King, Sean Rogers, Lu Butler, Andrew Kilbride, David Wilson, Curtis Shop, Cameron Schroy, Jesse Blank, Joe Antonwitz, Ryan Kerney, Ed Deitsch, Montana Hartman, Michael Pederson, Jacob Morgan, Don Palesky, and Kathleen Pratt! (More announcements may be forthcoming.)
And in New York City and the states of New Jersey and Virginia, history was made!
Congratulations to Mamdani, Sherrill, and Spansburger for their historic wins!
We didn't win everything, but we took the major races.
And next year, we will [#ChooseDemocracy](https://bethfarnhamforcongress.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=4f4a1a13014eb630c91960b1cba273b5d8e2667955e6b62001f96bd9915c9da5&blog_id=213069660&post_id=5491&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=1dfec748f1907fe19b100dd168f2a8c0&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=2c242be240de29cb973db19e3e4c3afc) and take back our Congress!
You may have noticed that I am updating some things on my website. For example, at the top of each page, there is an ability to return to the home page by clicking on my picture, or you can click on a menu that includes Priorities, Meet Beth, Contact Beth, DONATE!, and Join Us!. There is also a search bar on the home page for you to search terms or issues important to you. Many thanks to Spencer McLaughlin for helping me with this project!
Recently, I updated my priorities on Democracy and Reproductive Freedom, plus I added Affordability as another priority. I will also be updating my other priorities, mostly to clarify my pledges to you as our US Representative. It's also important for you to know who I am so I offer to you my updated bio titled, "Meet Beth."
Dear Voter,
As your candidate, I am honored and privileged to run again for the office of US Representative for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District. It means everything to me to be a voice in Congress for our precious Democracy, Reproductive Freedom, Affordability, Gun Safety, Public Education, Universal Healthcare, Immigration Reform, the Environment, and more. And it all begins with representation that is real and accessible.
I’m not some multi-millionaire, but a stay-at-home parent, ready to return to the paid workforce. This position has afforded me the ability to meet with voters like you across our stunningly beautiful district in the counties of Adams, Franklin, Fulton, Bedford, Cambria, Blair, Huntingdon, Mifflin, Juniata, Perry, Cumberland, and Somerset and I have the tremendous support of my family, starting with my husband, Rob.
Rob and I married at Gettysburg Presbyterian Church almost 20 years ago and settled in Adams County, where we bought our home and are raising our children who attend the local public schools. We also grew together in our political journey from bumbling Republicans who didn’t really pay attention to the issues to the dedicated Democrats we are today. We raise our children to recognize that “All...are created EQUAL,” that when we realize each other’s right to “life, liberty, and the pursuit of happiness,” we love our neighbors as ourselves. Our exuberant dog and loud, fluffy cat round out our energetic family.
I don’t just talk about Everyday Americans, I am one, expecting that our tax policies benefit us, not the billionaires. I pinch pennies, recycle, drive a used hybrid car, and own one blue suit. I care about Freedom and the future of our children. As the child of an HVAC technician and a nurse, I spent most of my childhood in a row home in Baltimore and I always attended public schools. I put myself through college, and was the only member of my immediate family to graduate from it. I am forever grateful to my first grade teacher, Mrs. Thomas, who taught me how to read and write because I would not be where I am today without her support and guidance.
I’ve been a credit analyst, an administrative assistant for a lift company, a school board member, and a pharmacy technician. With a degree in Biology, French, and Secondary Education, I like to say I’ve studied life from its basic beginnings to its sophisticated ends. I’m not just trying to represent us, I’ve walked many of the same paths as you – from living on my own, to raising a family, to dealing with aging family, to holding a job, to being a public servant. And all of that is exactly what prepares me to run for this office.
I love to meet people, to listen to what’s on your hearts and minds, then take action. I’ve marched, delivered speeches, and created informational slideshows because I believe in tackling important issues like defending our sacred right to vote, recognizing that Black Lives Matter, advocating clear paths to citizenship, promoting responsible gun ownership, having our healthcare needs covered, affording our basic necessities, advancing agency over our own bodies, expanding public education, and so much more in ways that broaden understanding and educate others.
As our candidate and US Representative, I will never forsake the sacrifices that every veteran, union member, and activist who ever marched in the name of Democracy ever made. I am proud to continue their fights to improve our American lives. You can find my specific pledges as our US Representative under each topic on my [Priorities](https://bethfarnhamforcongress.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=940c0d3b9df1c779734f751baed9eb800c8a2fbeb2477d8228bd7563ebb02fb9&blog_id=213069660&post_id=5491&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=9e529bea11abfbc2c65e1380e2150341&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly9iZXRoZmFybmhhbWZvcmNvbmdyZXNzLmNvbS9wcmlvcml0aWVzLw=&email_id=2c242be240de29cb973db19e3e4c3afc) page.
Last year, over 104,000 of you voted for me, for which I am entirely grateful. I am building upon those votes, continuing to knock on doors, call voters, hold in-person town halls, man tents at your local festivals, march in your local parades, speak to members of local organizations, reach out across social media, and respond to your heartfelt emails.
My team is growing bigger, stronger, faster, so please join us if you would like to help make this 2026 win happen. In the most important election of our lives, I ask you to please #ChooseDemocracy and vote for me, Beth Farnham, to represent us.
A vote for me is a vote for the American ideals of individual worth, equal rights, liberty, enfranchisement, and majority rule, once promised by the Revolutionary Founders, improved by the marching generations, and manifested in you.
Sincerely,
Beth
Last Saturday, I I attended the play, "Miss Lydia's Church" at the Melody Theater of Cross Keys Village in New Oxford.
I [](https://bethfarnhamforcongress.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=e305db9dd84789c42553f0b19e0917196cb24aa03095e4af64e7fa832c0e14f5&blog_id=213069660&post_id=5491&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=c2816994c9545a713baf3b68409efa5a&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url&email_id=2c242be240de29cb973db19e3e4c3afc)appreciated the message of the play and the dialogue afterward with playwright Dorothy King.
May we always have the important conversations about race relations.
Thank you, Pathways to Racial Justice for promoting it!
If you’re like me, you love this country as much as I do and work to make a positive difference. In the midst of this Constitutional Crisis, please consider reaching out to your local Democratic committees or Democratic candidates so that we can strengthen our Democracy instead of letting it slide into fascism.
We don’t just #ChooseDemocracy, we choose Pennsylvania, we choose The United States of America, we choose Humanity and I am deeply glad to work along side you.
Sincerely,
Beth

name: Elise Stefanik
subject: I’m running for Governor
body:
I need 10,000 Day One Endorsements
| [View in Web Browser](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e9990630/VaHR0cHM6Ly93cm8ubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3YvbVNGN004bUt3Qw/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTExMDk/LbmNsZ2tCU1FyeQ/qP2hpZHM9bmNwTEJTZzM1aFF6TkVscw/gaREpMg/semaf371b11)
| | | [](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e9990630/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUud2lucmVkLmNvbS9ob3VzZS1nb3AtYmF0dGxlZ3JvdW5kLWZ1bmQvaGdiZl9kb25fb21fZXJfbGEtbnlnX3JzXzIwMjUxMTA3Lw/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTExMDk/LbmNsZ2tCU1FyeQ/qP3JlY3VycmluZz10cnVlJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09YnImdXRtX3Rlcm09MjE4NThfd3Ix/gaREpMg/s1gd19d32dc)
---
**Patriot, I’m in.**
I’ve just announced my campaign for Governor of New York.

[ENDORSE ELISE](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e9990630/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUud2lucmVkLmNvbS9ob3VzZS1nb3AtYmF0dGxlZ3JvdW5kLWZ1bmQvaGdiZl9kb25fb21fZXJfbGEtbnlnX3JzXzIwMjUxMTA3Lw/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTExMDk/LbmNsZ2tCU1FyeQ/qP3JlY3VycmluZz10cnVlJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09YnImdXRtX3Rlcm09MjE4NThfd3Ix/gaREpMg/s1gd19d32dc)
The tide is turning in the Empire State and the numbers show that voters are ready for a change:

[](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e9990630/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUud2lucmVkLmNvbS9ob3VzZS1nb3AtYmF0dGxlZ3JvdW5kLWZ1bmQvaGdiZl9kb25fb21fZXJfbGEtbnlnX3JzXzIwMjUxMTA3Lw/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTExMDk/LbmNsZ2tCU1FyeQ/qP3JlY3VycmluZz10cnVlJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09YnImdXRtX3Rlcm09MjE4NThfd3Ix/gaREpMg/s1gd19d32dc)
[](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e9990630/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUud2lucmVkLmNvbS9ob3VzZS1nb3AtYmF0dGxlZ3JvdW5kLWZ1bmQvaGdiZl9kb25fb21fZXJfbGEtbnlnX3JzXzIwMjUxMTA3Lw/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTExMDk/LbmNsZ2tCU1FyeQ/qP3JlY3VycmluZz10cnVlJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09YnImdXRtX3Rlcm09MjE4NThfd3Ix/gaREpMg/s1gd19d32dc)
**That’s right.****Recent polling shows me LEADING the worst Governor in America Kathy Hochul, and I am just getting started.**
Patriot, the stakes couldn’t be higher... AOC, Zohran Mamdani, and Kathy Hochul are going to destroy New York **[if we don’t stand and fight to stop them together](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e9990630/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUud2lucmVkLmNvbS9ob3VzZS1nb3AtYmF0dGxlZ3JvdW5kLWZ1bmQvaGdiZl9kb25fb21fZXJfbGEtbnlnX3JzXzIwMjUxMTA3Lw/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTExMDk/LbmNsZ2tCU1FyeQ/qP3JlY3VycmluZz10cnVlJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09YnImdXRtX3Rlcm09MjE4NThfd3Ix/gaREpMg/s1gd19d32dc)**.

**Patriot, that’s where you come in.** I am humbly asking for your Day One Endorsement.

name: Kerry McQuisten (Newsletter)
subject: Pretty Busy for a Government Shutdown!
body:
| | | | |
# **Republican Women Successes ... and Back to D.C. Soon**
---
---
I left off during my last newsletter at the Baker County Republican Women's (BCRW) annual banquet. Huge success. We now have enough funds to put on our Patriot Kids Camp at last. Real American history for your littles! Stay tuned. The next step is getting enough volunteers next summer to pull it off properly. We filled the Elks Lodge with our community for dinner, music, an auction, and an assortment of speakers. Brenda Coley from John Day catered - and I think I have her secret baked potato topping recipe almost figured out! Frank Carlson provided the music. Nate Shelman, Boise radio personality, emceed. We heard from Brigadier General (ret.) Blaine Holt, political expert on Brazil Julio Monteiro, and Rep. Mark Owens.
BCRW was also instrumental in funding and supporting the chief petitioners of a county-wide ballot initiative that gave voters in Baker County a say in upcoming event center projects over a million dollars IF they will raise taxes or seize/bulldoze residences. Two of our three allegedly Republican county commissioners are hellbent on moving forward with an event center/parking lot/road project that I predict will operate at a loss, raise taxes, take the homes of several residents and potentially pave over an historic cemetery. (Where all the bodies are buried is still quite nebulous. Have these people not seen _Poltergeist_!?) I could be wrong. But I doubt it. Aside from those county commissioners and their biz dev guy, the owners of a couple building companies, some of the PCPs who make up the Baker County GOP, and Baker County's exactly 23 far-left Democrats, we had very little opposition. In the end, the initiative passed with about 84.5% support. A blowout. A mandate. In short, the BCRW kicked butt on behalf of our people.
_Eh hem._ I should remind some folks that conservative values include personal responsibility, self-sufficiency, smaller government, and less spending.
Now back to D.C.
Literally.
With the government shutdown, a lot of the advocacy Solomon and I have been working on for Republicans Overseas naturally slowed in some areas, but I'm amazed how much has continued to move forward.
In October, the Senate finally confirmed a large swath of nominees who had been sitting on hold for months. This means _many _swearing-in ceremonies at the end of November. Following that, the White House is reopening more around President Trump's big construction project and scheduling several public and not-so-public events. We've been invited to several events that are simply too difficult to get back and forth from Oregon to in a short time frame. So, after a call from the White House a little over a week ago, we accepted one pretty cool invitation that will allow us to bring my 14-year-old daughter with us. We can't make it to the swearings-in, but we can take some Senate meetings and two meetings inside the State Department this time. We'll go from there.
More after Thanksgiving (for which I still haven't come up with a solid plan yet)! With that, a very happy Thanksgiving to you and yours.
_Kerry_
PS - For all you Republicans Overseas folks who read this newsletter, congratulations to Amb. Michael DeSombre. I know we'll all be with him in spirit, if not there physically, as SoS Rubio swears him in.
---
| |
---

clean_body: | | | | | Republican Women Successes ... and Back to D.C. Soon I left off during my last newsletter at the Baker County Republican Women's (BCRW) annual banquet. Huge success. We now have enough funds to put on our Patriot Kids Camp at last. Real American history for your littles! Stay tuned. The next step is getting enough volunteers next summer to pull it off properly. We filled the Elks Lodge with our community for dinner, music, an auction, and an assortment of speakers. Brenda Coley from John Day catered - and I think I have her secret baked potato topping recipe almost figured out! Frank Carlson provided the music. Nate Shelman, Boise radio personality, emceed. We heard from Brigadier General (ret.) Blaine Holt, political expert on Brazil Julio Monteiro, and Rep. Mark Owens. BCRW was also instrumental in funding and supporting the chief petitioners of a county-wide ballot initiative that gave voters in Baker County a say in upcoming event center projects over a million dollars IF they will raise taxes or seize/bulldoze residences. Two of our three allegedly Republican county commissioners are hellbent on moving forward with an event center/parking lot/road project that I predict will operate at a loss, raise taxes, take the homes of several residents and potentially pave over an historic cemetery. (Where all the bodies are buried is still quite nebulous. Have these people not seen Poltergeist!?) I could be wrong. But I doubt it. Aside from those county commissioners and their biz dev guy, the owners of a couple building companies, some of the PCPs who make up the Baker County GOP, and Baker County's exactly 23 far-left Democrats, we had very little opposition. In the end, the initiative passed with about 84.5% support. A blowout. A mandate. In short, the BCRW kicked butt on behalf of our people. Eh hem. I should remind some folks that conservative values include personal responsibility, self-sufficiency, smaller government, and less spending. Now back to D.C. Literally. With the government shutdown, a lot of the advocacy Solomon and I have been working on for Republicans Overseas naturally slowed in some areas, but I'm amazed how much has continued to move forward. In October, the Senate finally confirmed a large swath of nominees who had been sitting on hold for months. This means many swearing-in ceremonies at the end of November. Following that, the White House is reopening more around President Trump's big construction project and scheduling several public and not-so-public events. We've been invited to several events that are simply too difficult to get back and forth from Oregon to in a short time frame. So, after a call from the White House a little over a week ago, we accepted one pretty cool invitation that will allow us to bring my 14-year-old daughter with us. We can't make it to the swearings-in, but we can take some Senate meetings and two meetings inside the State Department this time. We'll go from there. More after Thanksgiving (for which I still haven't come up with a solid plan yet)! With that, a very happy Thanksgiving to you and yours. Kerry PS - For all you Republicans Overseas folks who read this newsletter, congratulations to Amb. Michael DeSombre. I know we'll all be with him in spirit, if not there physically, as SoS Rubio swears him in. | | Past Newsletters ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ | | The podcast is picking up listeners and advertisers!
